name: Katie Rosenblatt
intro: Hi everyone!! My name is Katie I'm from Scarsdale, NY, about thirty minutes outside of NYC. I am a second year, studying Business Administration with a concentration in Finance. last year I spent my first semester in Boston and my second semester in London. I love to travel and some of my favorite places I went to are Greece, Portugal, and Amsterdam. I highly recommend going abroad if you're thinking about it, it was such an amazing experience and, if possible, I definitely want to go abroad again. I am always listening to music (mostly rap and house but I like a little bit of everything) and some of my favorite artists are Travis Scott, Dom Dolla, A Boogie, Kid Cudi, and Don Toliver who I'm seeing for the second time next month! I'm looking to go to more concerts in Boston this year, so I am always down to go with any of you! I'm currently rewatching Modern Family but I have a serious New Girl obsession. I also love Breaking Bad and refused to watch TV for months after finishing it because I knew nothing would ever top it. In my free time I love to go thrifting, explore Boston, go swimming (I've spent the past three summers as a swim instructor), cook, and especially eat. I love hanging out with my friends and doing absolutely anything, so lmk when you want to hang. Many of you have probably heard me talk about my family's new puppy, Nash. He is a Golden retriever and I FaceTime my Mom at least five times a day so I can see him. I apologize in advance for spamming you with photos of him. I am so excited to be a part of TAMID and I can't wait to meet all of you!

name: Kaya Lee
intro: Hi everyone! my name is Kaya Lee. I am from the ocean city Busan in Korea (yes, the movie train to Busan)! I am a freshman, majoring in Economics and Business but trying to determine my concentration between marketing and finance. I used to play taekwondo for about five years and love going to the gym. Especially shoulder and back, they're my favorite spots to train. I am usually in Mario in the morning so hit me up if you are there! We shared our bucket lists in education session yesterday so I will throw some out here too in the hope that they will come true. Retire at the age of 25, Have a convenient store in my house, 10 pull ups. Fun fact about me is that I don't know how to swim still.. I am deadly afraid of water. I almost died when I went to Miami Beach because I didn't know how the sandbar worked that time and I just started panicking when I couldn't reach the floor. You might wonder how I end up being in Boston all the way from Korea. A little more about my background, I first learned my English in Philippines, went to an exchange program in California in middle school, went to highschool in Canada for a year, and graduated in a boarding school in Pennsylvania this spring. Yep.. I know I was all over the places. And this is why I love being in a foreign country and learning the new world. I am thrilled to be in Boston this time and can't wait to make another journey at Northeastern with you guys! Speaking of Boston, I love Levain bakery with a passion. It is the best way to rep up the week and I must get it every time. Lastly, I wanted to tell you that the face time call you guys did to those who got in was the sweetest thing ever! I am really looking forward to meeting you all in person in the upcoming weeks. Let's go TAMIDDD

name: Christy Pigott
intro: Hey, I'm Christy and I am from Ireland. Well, I feel as though some context is needed, I grew up in rural Ireland (farm country) then moved to Dublin before my family decided to spontaneously move to Madrid where I spent my first two years of high school (and learned Spanish). To finish off, I spent the last two years living in London, while 7 schools seems drastic, how it has shaped me is truly invaluable. Now that my lengthy background story has been covered, I can go back to the basics. I am a first year, Economics and International Business Major (concentration maybe in Finance, might dabble in a data science or philosophy minor). Being the sole place I have ever ventured cross Atlantic, Boston is one of my favourite places. For me, it encapsulates everything I loved about Madrid and London in one place. It has the bustling energy of London, everyone here is always moving and busy going from one place to another, with a European feel which I cannot fully describe, nonetheless I feel completely at peace here. To say my interests lie on a broad spectrum would be an understatement, I'll start with my love for being up high, more specifically whether that be on mountain peaks or skyscrapers, it is somewhere I find utter tranquility. With that, I am a true ski fanatic, my family and I take it very seriously (quite a funny scene actually) and thanks to my roots, I am in fact a horse girl (one of my dreams is live in a mountainous region, owning my own horse). I love listening to music, ranging from Reggaeton (when I miss Spain), to Afro beats (reminds me of London), Techno, EDM (concerts = yes), also love instrumental music after playing piano for last 10 years (enemies to lovers relationship here). I will leave you with one fun fact about myself, I can drive a tractor but don't have my car drivers licence, yet, I am starting lessons shortly though. On the topic of learning new things, I am currently working on my constant stoic facial expression, pls reach out with tips. I know first hand only so much can be said in words, so I'm beyond excited to be apart of this incredible community at TAMID and to meet you all in person.

name: Rajvi Shah
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Rajvi and I'm from Long Island, NY. So I get the city during the winter and the Hamptons (even though I can't afford it) for the the summer. I'm a second year studying Business Administration with a concentration in Finance and a minor in emerging markets. I'm excited to get involved in the finance track. I love doing my nails, thrifting, cooking, and going to the gym. So if you ever want to support my VERY UNPROFESSIONAL small business, shop with a personal stylist, cook in my small dav b kitchen, or finally force me to hit arms and upper body, I would love it!! Besides that, I love to take advantage of this GORGEOUS city by shopping through Newbury street, eating through the north end, and walking by the Charles river. Basically anywhere where I won't get hit by a wind tunnel… I also love to stay in and be a bum. I could watch one of my comfort shows for the hundredth time (new girl, the good place, or Jane the virgin), listen to some of my favorite artists that range from Sabrina Carpenter to Travis Scott, or talk to my roommates about nothing for hours. Im so excited to get to know you all.

name: Jacques van der Merwe
intro: Hey everyone, My name is Jacques and I'm from Connecticut. I was originally born near Cape Town, South Africa and I moved to the states 13 years ago. I'm a second year studying Business Admin with a concentration in Finance and Data Science. I spent my first semester here in Boston and my second semester in London, which was super fun. I travelled to Portugal, France, Scotland, Belgium, Switzerland, and Denmark. My favorite place is a tie between Copenhagen and London. I rowed throughout high school and I now love fitness and nutrition, but I also love eating and cooking. I have an apartment with a kitchen this year, so I've been chefing up. I also love finding good food around Boston, so please hit me up with your restaurant recs! I also love cars, animals, and music. What I'm listening to is always changing but I love rap, house, classic, country, reggae, and bossa nova. Right now, I'm listening to a lot of rap, mainly Travis Scott. I'm a huge F1 fan. My favorite driver is Charles Leclerc and (was) Daniel Riccardo. My favorite team is Ferrari although they are incredibly frustrating to watch. I don't watch a lot of TV but my favorite show is Game of Thrones. It's a hard to choose a favorite movie, but it's probably James Bond: Skyfall. Super excited to be a part of TAMID and I can't wait to meet everyone!

name: Rikin Rakesh
intro: Hi everyone!! My name is Rikin Rakesh and I'm from Malvern, Pennsylvania - 30 minutes from Philadelphia. I am a second year studying Computer Science and Business with a concentration in Finance. I have been in Boston all my semesters so far, although I sometimes wish I went abroad for a semester after hearing all the NUin kids rave about their experiences. I am a huge sports enthusiast. When it comes to football, basketball, and baseball, Philly teams have my heart. So...as much as I love the city of Boston, you'll never catch me rooting for the Celtics or Patriots. My favorite sport is soccer and I've played it all of my life, including here at college. Funny enough, as much as I love soccer, I rarely, if ever, watch the Philadelphia Union. I'm more into the Premier League and LaLiga. One of my dreams is to watch a Champions League game in person in Europe. Besides sports, I also love music. I listen to a variety of artists, but a couple of my favorites right now are The Weeknd, Lana del Rey, and The Neighbourhood. Some of my other hobbies include working out, thrifting, and watching TV shows. I am currently watching Game of Thrones which is also insane. I'm a little ashamed to say I haven't been exploring Boston as much as I want to, so please drop recs on things to do or places to visit that are fun because I have a friend coming next weekend and I need to seem interesting. I am beyond excited to have the opportunity to join TAMID's community and I can't wait to get to know all of you.

name: Pearl Gupta
intro: Hi everyone! I'm Pearl Gupta, from Long Island, New York. I'm a sophomore studying Finance and Accounting. I spent my first year in Oakland (still love Cali!), but Boston has been really cool—feels young and lively. I'm obsessed with sunsets at the Charles River and really want to bike around there one day. At home, I'm super into MMA and airplanes—my dad and I go flying pretty often. Oh, and I'm probably the biggest matcha fan ever. If you know any good spots or want to grab matcha sometime, I'm so down! I love going out on weekends and dressing up (even though I'm usually in sweats, let's be real). But I'm also into staying in and chilling with friends. I love traveling with my parents, especially road trips! Europe is one of my favorite spots to go around. I really want to go to Japan one day though to see the tea gardens. I'm also on the New England Bhangra Team and absolutely love dancing. My music taste is all over the place—from pop and R&B to… yes, even country. I'm also into lifting, so if you're ever up for an early morning gym session, let me know! Can't wait to be apart of TAMID and meet everyone!

name: Lauren Meserve
intro: Hey everyone!! I'm Lauren. I'm a second year majoring in International Business with concentrations in brand management and finance. Im interested in the consulting and fund tracks. I'm originally from Los Angeles and went to an all girls high school (I LOVED it). I was a global scholar meaning I spent my first semester in Boston and second semester in London during my first year. London is honestly my favorite city so having this opportunity was SO amazing. I travelled to Copenhagen, Stockholm, Amsterdam, Dublin, Amalfi, and Rome. But my favorite trip was going to the arctic circle in Finland with my parents during spring break. We went husky sledding under the northern lights and on a reindeer sleigh ride. I grew up as a rhythmic gymnast and that was pretty much my entire life up until college. I had some of the coolest opportunities while being a gymnast. I performed at the Emmy's after party (met Zendaya and Sandra Oh), opened in the Special Olympics opening ceremony when they were in LA, and performed at a UCLA gymnastics meet. Now, I like to dance, do Pilates or yoga, and play pickleball. I love trying new restaurants and matcha spots and going to concerts. I'm going to Billie Eilish next week and I've never been more excited. I'm currently watching Sex and the City but am almost finished so I'm in desperate need of tv show recs. I am SOOOO excited to join TAMID and can't wait to meet all of you.

name: Augustine Vu
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Augustine Vu and I am a second year student studying Bioengineering. I recently transferred from Wentworth which is right down the street. I'm from Newton, MA which is about 20 minutes out from Boston, so I am pretty familiar with the city. I love watching UFC, Boxing, and occasionally F1. One of my dream is to see each of those sports live in person! I love cooking food, and more importantly eating food! My favorite foods are sushi, ramen, and pho. I have also have a huge sweet tooth, so I also love eating ice cream, candy, and boba. I grew up doing Taekwondo but I also wrestled in high school but unfortunately that didn't last long due to an injury. I love going to the gym so if you ever need a gym buddy feel free to reach out! I listen to a lot of pop, kpop, and rap. Some of my favorite artists include: The Weeknd, Travis Scott, Wave to Earth, Jackson Wang, Hojean, and Emotional Oranges. Please let me know if you have any music recommendations! I am extremely excited to join TAMID and cant wait to meet all of you guys!!

name: Miya Weintraub
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Miya Weintraub (pronounced Me-yuh), and I'm from the Washington DC area—more specifically, Northern Virginia (NoVA). I'm 3/4 Japanese and 1/4 German. I'm studying Finance and Fintech, although for more than a semester now, I've been contemplating whether or not I should add a DS minor. Please let me know if you have any insights! Career-wise, I want to go into investment banking and, hopefully, private equity in the future. I love competing, so naturally, I'm really into sports. I've played soccer since I was 5 up until my senior year of high school, I started climbing over the summer, and I rode the pickleball wave. In general, I like being active, and I will rarely say no to any sort of physical activity. I love the outdoors and am always down for an adventure. I love to photograph/record my adventures, and I even have a YouTube channel (@miyaweintraub) where I post my content, although I haven't been active recently. I use a Canon Powershot G7X Mark III for all my photos and videos—the classic point-and-shoot—and I am obsessed with it, so I highly recommend it. I was also in Scouts, so I've been on many backpacking trips, campouts, and hikes, and I just love being in the wilderness. Also, fun fact: I have 2 brothers and 4 step-brothers, and there are no other girls in my family. Both of my brothers have their PPLs (private pilot's license), along with my dad, so getting my PPL is one of my big goals for the future. I often go flying with my dad, so I do know a little bit about that. I also have a dog that I'm absolutely obsessed with, named Toby. He's a rescue, and he's so precious to me. I also love to travel, and my favorite places that I've been to are Japan and Iceland. I love to cook (and eat, of course), but recently I've been a bit lazy and haven't been cooking as much. I'm so excited to be a part of the TAMID community, and I can't wait to meet everyone!!

name: Pujan Desai
intro: Hello everyone! My name is Pujan Desai and I'm a second year Computer Science & Business Administration major with a concentration in Finance. I'm from Old Bridge, NJ (like 20 minutes away from Rutgers). I'm interested in the Tech Consulting track. I love to hike and travel. Over the summer, I visited Spain and France with my family and I love it. Both countries are super beautiful and have a lot to offer. Some of the other places I've visited include India, Singapore, Honduras, Malaysia, Cozumel, etc. As much as I like to be outdoors, I also like to be indoors as well in my bed watching shows and listening to music. Some of my favorite shows include The Blacklist, Suits, Breaking Bad, The Boys, and Ozark. My music taste is pretty diverse. I listen to mainly Hindi and Punjabi music but some of my favorite artists and bands include Arjit Singh, Sukha, Diljit, Drake, Brent, Kendrick, J Cole, Cigarettes After Sex, Tame Impala, The Weeknd, Daniel Caesar, SZA, etc. I'm a huge baseball, basketball, and tennis fan. I played a lot of baseball when I was younger and played varsity tennis in high school. I play basketball and pickleball with my friends for fun as well. I also enjoy working out so you can find me at Marino almost everyday. I do mainly strength training so if you want to be put through a brutal workout, yk who to hit up. I'm super excited to be a part of the TAMID community and I can't wait to meet you all.

name: George Kressler
intro: Hi everyone! My name is George Kressler, I am from Rumson, NJ, which is right across from NYC, so about a 30 min ferry boat ride from my house! I am a second year student, and I just transferred to Northeastern from the University of San Diego. I loved my time there, but am happy now, being closer to home. I am studying Computer Science and Business Administration with a concentration in FinTech. I love indoor rock climbing. I got into it heavily in San Diego, and I was thinking about doing the club here but I have class conflicts most nights so if anyone here climbs let me know! I saw Kaya and a few others share some of their bucket list ideas from the meeting and I loved that so here are a few of mine too (only oddly specific ones allowed): Learn to ride a horse, but then ride one in France on the Normandy beaches. Meet Post Malone (my favorite music artist) and give him my converse and take his. Take a photo (I love photography) that gets used in the New York Times, or some other large publication. Later in life, go to paramedic school just to learn. And a couple more little facts about me, I am part of my hometown's volunteer EMS squad (60%+ of firefighters and EMS personnel in the US are volunteers) and am super involved there when I am home. I respond to about 200-300 calls a year and have had a lot of really impactful experiences doing this, and I highly suggest other people see about doing this in their towns because chances are your department needs more members! I love photography, all types but I really take a lot of film pics, so chances are if you're out with me I am bringing my film camera haha. I also love Formula 1, my favorite driver was Daniel Ricardio, but my backup favorite has always been George Russell. And one more thing is I make music! I have done it under a lot of names since I started when I was in 5th grade, but you can find my recent stuff under the name "georgie." on spotify and apple music etc, and I have an EP coming next friday (oct 11)! I am from a family of 6 (including my parents and me), with a huge extended family on my moms side, so family and community is really important to me and I am so excited to join TAMID to find that community at NEU. My brother is a quadriplegic (paralyzed) from a mountain biking accident in 2020, and so I am super involved in local charities, and specifically I do some emergency web development stuff to help families direct community support to areas where they can donate in the immediate hours after an accident. With those projects I've raised over $150,000 for my own family, and helped other families raise over $6,000,000 for their loved ones. Sorry this message is so long! But I am a very visual person so for those like me I attached a few pics to show ya who I am in some ways. Lastly, I am so excited to get involved in TAMID, and meet all of you. Thank you for giving me this opportunity. Please feel free to reach out to me at any time!

name: Catherine McKinley
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Catherine McKinley and I'm a second year. I'm from northern Virginia and I'm majoring in Data Science and Mathematics. I spent my first year studying in London which was so much fun and I got to travel all around Europe which was an amazing experience, but I'm super excited to be in Boston! I enjoy reading, trying new workout classes, and running! I also enjoy yoga and I've been loving CorePower lately. I used to work at a boutique yoga studio in my hometown and interacting with so many students and teachers all the time really grew my love for the practice. I also love picking up random hobbies here and there to try new things. This summer I started playing a lot of pickleball with my parents and we've been having a lot of fun with it. I also love a basic long walk with a good podcast or a friend to chat with! I love watching a good romcom or rewatching either Friends or Gilmore Girls. I also have a music taste that's all over the place. My favorite artist is Stephen Sanchez, but I'm really into house music right now! I'm so excited to be a part of the TAMID community and I can't wait to get to know you all better!!

name: Jeff Krapf
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Jeff, I'm majoring in Data Science and Economics, and I'm from a small town in Ohio near the city where Lebron is from (Akron). I've lived in that town for my whole life until I made the big decision to go to a boarding school...which also happened to be in the small town I grew up in. It was a very memorable experience, but I'm definitely super happy that I'm in Boston now! I absolutely love this city. I especially enjoy wandering through the esplanade at night and exploring the city through the day. Last year, I was in Greece through the NUin program. It was such an amazing experience and I was able to cross off so many of my bucket list items. I traveled to Vienna, Zermatt, Amsterdam, Malta, Budapest, and Sofia in Bulgaria. I honestly can't choose a favorite between those place as each hold such a special place in my heart. I also love getting outside and playing pickup sports whenever I get the chance, soccer, basketball, spikeball, pickleball, and tennis (which I'm absolutely terrible at). Unfortunately, I dislocated my shoulder and tore my labrum on a spikeball serve. Not only is it an incredibly embarrassing story, but the tear is severe enough that I'm getting surgery on it in December so I'll "technically" be out of commision for a few months. I am a HUGE fan of Cleveland Sports which also unfortunately means I get very sad on Sundays in the fall. I also love keeping up to date with soccer in Europe, specifically tottenham. Beyond sports, I love all things related to music. Recently, I've been into a lot of Steely Dan, The Beatles, Blondie, and Berlioz, but I listen to anything from jam bands to my roommate who's a "dj" set. I'm always looking for new music to listen too so I'd be more than happy to listen to your recommendation's. I am absolutely pumped to be a part of Tamid community and I am so excited to meet everyone!!

name: Chewie Wachs
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Matthew Wachs but I've had the nickname "Chewie" my entire life. I am a second year Business Administration student from Ardsley, NY with a concentration in Finance and two possible minors in Data Sciences and Industrial Engineering. Last year I was NUin Rome before arriving in Boston for the Spring sem. Additionally, I've been lucky to travel to Israel, Germany, England and other places with my loving 7 person family. I'm a HUGE movie buff. I'll bounce from watching the Social Network to How to Train Your Dragon back to Life Is Beautiful and then to The Other Guys. A couple of my favorite movies include Whiplash, Reservoir Dogs, Back to the Future, and The Grand Budapest Hotel. I am raging Tarantino enthusiast with a hot take being that Once Upon A Time In Hollywood is dookie (sorry not sorry). In all seriousness, hmu if you wanna watch any movies and follow my letterboxd @PunchItChewie (shameless plug). Aside from movies, I love listing to music while I complete random athletic challenges like Jiu Jitsu and triathlons. My favorite hip hop musical artists at the moment are Mac Miller, Childish Gambino and Frank Ocean, yet I also switch it up by listening to the Red Hot Chili Peppers, Eagles, Beastie Boys and U2. I am super excited and can't wait to meet everyone in TAMID!

name: Tyler Hwang
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Tyler Hwang and I am a second year majoring in finance with a minor in DS. I am from LA. In my opinion the best place and better than the Bay Area in my opinion lol. A fun fact is that my graduating class of my high school only had 23 people lol. I do really enjoy Boston, I've been here since first semester and I am excited to explore even more of Boston. I am a big back lol. I love to eat, so if anybody wants to eat, let me know. I have grown up playing all kinds of sports from soccer, basketball, baseball, and volleyball. I am always down to get active. I actually did recently just join the club basketball team this year. You guys will probably catch me playing at marino all the time lol. If anyone wants to hoop or even gym with me let me know lol. Besides sports, I really enjoy taking nice pictures and going to scenic areas. I also am really interested in fashion and am very curious to explore more about fashion. So if anybody wants to go shop for clothes or take nice fit pictures. If anyone needs nice pictures for their instagram, let me know. I have been professionally trained by my sister. I am really excited for TAMID and I cannot wait to meet everyone!!

name: Sahaj Baxi
intro: Hi guys! I am Sahaj Baxi and I am a second year studying Computer Science and Finance. I am from Mumbai India, and I have been in Boston since last year. According to me, from the few cities I think I can live at, except for Mumbai, has to be Boston (because of the sea). I absolutely love to go on long walks with my friends, either to just wander around without any intention, or to have a clear and specific goal in mind (to get food!), or any other random thing. Whenever someone wants to have a good long walk, you know just the person to reach out to. I also love to workout and go to the gym regularly, but that does not stop me from enjoying sports. I am big fan of playing sports rather than watching them, even though I'm not the best at them. I just enjoy playing when I'm with my friends and it can be anything from outdoor (basketball, soccer, cricket) to indoor (table tennis). I equally like to go out and stay in. I love to explore and discover new lanes, streets, food places, go on meaningless drives and other activities, but I also need my indoor activities such as a good long yap session with my friends, or a nice movie night, or some other games like cards, or 'would you rather'. I'm a huge fan of movies, and used to watch a lot of them in highschool, but the number drastically went down in college. Some of my favorite movies are interstellar, edge of tomorrow, black panther and zindagi na milegi dobara (its a must watch Bollywood film!). Other than this I also like to sketch and draw things, these include any doodling on my notebook, trying to get an accurate face portrait of my friends (or making their funny caricatures) to a full fledged sketch, mostly of animals. I see many people have posted their pets and sadly I donot have one yet. I aim to get my first puppy, right after graduating from college (golden retriever mostly!). I am so excited to meet all of you guys, and be a part of TAMID!

name: Ilay Zubkov
intro: Hey y'all! My name is Ilay (pronounced E-LIE) and I'm a second year computer science major. Have been recently thinking of going combined cs and business tho, and will def be asking people for insight and advice about that. I'm originally from Israel but moved to Brooklyn when I was 9, and then to Tenafly, NJ, when I was 12. Track wise, I'm interested in consulting and potentially fund. I love spending time outdoors and being active, whether it's going to the gym, playing basketball with friends, hiking, skiing, surfing, or anything else, I'm always down. Also love trying out new sports and activities, and have been wanting to get into pickleball recently. In general, I just enjoy new experiences and want to explore Boston more in my free time, which I feel like I haven't done enough of despite being here all of last year. Lmk if you have any recommendations for restaurants, places to visit, or other fun things to do in the city! I'm a massive music fan, and can listen to almost anything. I've started to learn how to DJ recently, so house and rap are definitely the genres I've been listening to the most lately. My all time favorite shows are the office, curb your enthusiasm, and South Park, but I like a super wide variety of shows and movies besides just comedies. Also, my family got a puppy a week before I moved back home for the summer, so I was able to hangout with her the whole time and I miss her sm. Her name is Milka, or Milki, and ik she's a crusty white dog but I will tolerate no disrespect bc she's the cutest and most energetic doggo I've ever seen. I could keep yapping on and on, but I'll finish this off and say I'm incredibly excited to get to know everyone, and am so happy to be here.

name: Danny Weinstein
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Danny Weinstein and I am from a small town in the Northwest suburbs of Chicago. I am a second year student studying economics and international business, concentrating in business analytics and fintech. I went on the Entrepreneurship and Global Consulting dialogue to South Korea this past summer, which has been the highlight of my Northeastern life so far. After that experience, I decided that I initially want to pursue the consulting trek and then potentially transition to fund. Even though I have lived in the same house in the same town my whole life, I had a very multicultural upbringing. My mom is from Russia and my dad is from Ukraine, and I was raised Jewish. My native language growing up was Russian, and I only started speaking English when I went into grade school. Since then I have also learned to speak Spanish fluently, and my goal is to learn French next. Speaking of goals, here are some of my unique bucket list items: Cook the national dish of every country, Go to every single F1 race in a year, Run a marathon on every continent, Own a home with a sauna and ice rink, Have enough time in my life to run for president, just for fun. I love food. I think life without good food is a life without any joy. My favorite cuisine is Japanese food, specifically sushi. But if I had to pick a final meal, it would have to be my grandma's Georgian food. Her khachapuri and khinkali are out of this world, better than any restaurant I have been to. My favorite restaurants that I have been to recently are Sarma (Sommerville) and Le Banquet Des Sophistes (Strasbourg). I also love cooking creative dishes in my apartment. Some of my best recipes from this semester include Thai green curry, Italian beef ragu, Japanese steak and broccoli, Bosnian lamb burek, and Turkish baklava. In my free time, I love watching sports, especially soccer, hockey, and tennis. My favorite soccer teams are Tottenham and Bayer Leverkusen, while my favorite hockey team is the Blackhawks. Finally, I am an avid traveler. I have been to 40 countries around the world, with my most memorable locations being Iceland, Guatemala, Morocco, and Portugal. I hope to keep exploring the world, with my bucket list destinations being Peru, Japan, and South Africa. So yeah, that's all about me. I am beyond excited to be a part of the TAMID community and to get to know everyone in the coming months.

name: Andrew Poehlmann
intro: Hey everyone, my name is Andrew and I'm from Vermont. I live about 20 minutes from our biggest city, which only has 40,000 people, so I've spent a lot of my life in a smaller rural community. I feel as if this was a defining factor that made me want to move to the city for college, and Boston felt like the perfect place to do so. I'm a second year studying History and Economics, with an interest in either pursuing law school or entering into private wealth management/financial management! Some of my biggest hobbies include playing and watching soccer, watching movies (which I could talk about forever), board games, spikeball, and building LEGOs. I also really like watching sports, and I'm a huge Boston sports fan, especially the Patriots and Celtics, both of whom I hope to get tickets to watch play at some point in their seasons. Another one of my favorite things to do is listen to music! I am a huge music enjoyer and I'd like to believe I'm pretty open to all genres and artists. Some of my favorite artists and musicians are Montell Fish, who I just saw in concert a couple weeks ago, Radiohead, Clairo, Pink Floyd, the Beatles, Beach House, A Tribe Called Quest, Kendrick Lamar, Sufjan Stevens, Mac Demarco, Adele, the Smiths, and Billy Joel. Some of my favorite concerts I've been to are Kendrick Lamar and Baby Keem, Daniel Caesar and Montell Fish, and Travis Scott. Lastly, when it comes to watching shows, my all time favorite TV series has to be Better Call Saul, however, Breaking Bad does come in a close second. Other shows that I've loved have been Mr. Robot, which I think is super underrated and would highly recommend to anyone looking for something new to watch, Game of Thrones, and Dexter!! I'm super excited at this amazing opportunity to join Tamid and get to know everyone here- I am looking forward to meeting you all!

name: Kristina Carandang
intro: Hi everyone!! I'm Kristina. I'm a third year majoring in Industrial Engineering with a minor in Computer Science. I'm from San Diego, and as much as I love the west coast I fear I'm slowly becoming an east coast girl. I've been in Boston for all of my semesters so far, but would love to study abroad at some point in the future. This year I've been trying to get more into reading, my goal is to read 1 book every month and so far I'm on schedule. I started the year with The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo, the ACOTAR series, the Fourth Wing series, and am now finishing The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo series. Next, I'm planning to read Harry Potter because I have yet to read any of the books or watch any of the movies. Following Kaya, George, and a some others, some of my bucket list items were: Sing Chasing Pavements at an Adele concert, Catch a baseball at a baseball game (preferably at a Padres game), See a flying squirrel. I enjoy doing anything active with my friends – pickleball, hiking, tennis. From ages 6-16 I was a competitive gymnast, and while my gymnastics days are long gone, this past summer my friends and I found an open gym that's for ages 18+. They have a foam pit, trampoline and everything! If anyone wants to go let me know it's every Wednesday night at 9 and I'm really good at spotting people on handstands. I also started an intramural soccer team this semester. I have little to no soccer experience but it's always been a dream of mine to score a goal. I randomly found soccer cleats the last time I was home, so naturally I brought them back and started a team where everyone's purpose was to set me up to score a goal (our team name is the caranDANGS). I'm happy to announce that last night, I scored my first ever soccer goal ever (it was a PK but it still counts). Lastly, I'm going to include a photo of the bagel sandwich I made this morning. It has nothing to do with anything I just said, I'm just really proud of it and wanted to share it with everyone. I'm so excited to be apart of TAMID and I'm even more excited to meet everyone!!

name: Maximus McElroy
intro: Hey everyone! I am Maximus but you guys can call me Max, and I'm a sophomore studying Finance and International Business. Im from Manhattan, NY lived there my whole life, and will always be a city kid. So far in my year and a half at Northeastern, I did NUin Paris and a DOC in South Korea. Both were amazing experiences and really grew my love for travel. Some of my favorite cities I visited this past year are, Seoul, Rome, Geneva, Paris, Reykjavik, Porto, and so many more I can't list them all! I also love solo traveling and many of the places I visited were on my own. Everyone has pretty cool fun facts or unique bucket list items so I thought I would mention few: Visit 100 countries, Play in the World Series of Poker main event (and hopefully win), Produce/Direct my own movie/film, Semi-master the piano. Just like many, I love cinema. Been a A-List amc member for 3 years and have had the privilege of living close to the AMC Lincon Square which has a 70mm IMax screen! Some of my favorite Movies are, Gladiator (Named after Maximus), The Godfather 2, Stand By Me, Back to the Future, Planet of the Apes, Monsters Inc, and Harry Potter. (Feel free to add me on Letterboxd @ MaximusMcelroy) For music I love hip hop and rock. Im a huge Weeknd fan and can't wait for the new album! I also grew up on 80s rock, some of my favorite bands are Guns'N Roses, Iron Maiden, and Ozzy Osbourne. Some other fun facts about me are that I am a huge sport fan (Yankees, Rangers, Knicks, and … Patriots). I love everything baseball related and was a big player growing up. In addition I LOVE hockey and ice skating. Ive skated and worked my whole life (part time now) at an Ice Rink in NYC as an assistant manager and lead Zamboni driver. Fun fact I don't know how to drive a car but I can really kill it on a Zamboni! Im also looking to get better at chess so I will play anyone anytime. Anyway I am super excited to be part of this community and I can't wait to meet everyone!

name: Vaishnavi Chebrolu
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Vaishnavi and I am a second year majoring in Biology and Computer Science! I'm from central mass (about an hour out from the city) and grew up visiting Boston very often. I've also been in Boston all of freshman year, but really want to study abroad at some point! When I was a kid, I played all sorts of sports, but the ones I really stuck with were soccer, swimming, and competitive archery! I don't play any in college, but love going to spin and yoga classes instead! Growing up in Mass means being around some of the best sports teams in the country. Some of my favorite memories are of my family heading into the city to watch the Celtics play! I'm a huge fan of food. Literally anything from Taco Bell to Ocean Prime brings me joy, but unfortunately I haven't learned to cook yet... hopefully having a kitchen this year will force me to learn! I also love listening to music! My favorite genres have to be R&B, house and rap! Right now I love listening to Lil Tecca, Black Coffee, Drake, and Brent Faiyaz (seeing him irl was insane). Zach Bryan is also slowly becoming one of my top artists!! Lastly, I love hanging out with my friends and going on day trips either into different parts of the city, or out of state to Newport! My favorite TV shows include New Girl, Suits and Gossip Girl! My top comfort movies have to be The Lion King and How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days. With all this said I literally haven't been able to find a new show to watch in like 2 months so please give me recs so I can stop re-watching New Girl. I am so so excited to join TAMID and can't wait to meet everyone!!

name: Daniel (CS/Finance)
intro: Hi everyone! I'm Daniel and I'm a second year Computer Science and Finance Major, and I'm thinking of adding a math minor. I'm from Bethesda Maryland which is just outside Washington DC. I haven't left the Boston campus since starting school, but I'm hoping to take advantage of some of the amazing global study opportunities sometime soon. Some of my hobbies are playing casual pickup sports. My favorites are probably baseball, (or any more casual variant like blitzball or softball), spikeball and football, but I will honestly try anything. I was also on my high school rowing team and while I don't do it much anymore I still like keeping up with major crew-related events. I have also gotten really into rock climbing in the last couple months and I go bouldering a few times a week (lmk if anyone wants to come I'm always looking for new people to go with). I also really like outdoor excursions and I try to go camping and/or hiking at a couple times a year. The same thing goes for skiing which I've been doing for around 5 years now. I also love any and every kind of music. I would try to name all my favorite artists here, but I think I would hit the slack message limit. I've been bumping a lot of jazz and 90s rap recently though, so I'll say Amy Winehouse and UGK are two of my top artists atm. I also play Piano, but I learned from mostly classical training and I want to get into more jazz theory so I can improv. I don't know if I can really call myself a foodie, but I do love eating. Especially when I can go out to get a lot of food for a good price. Professionally, I'm pretty techy and I really like to just grind out random project ideas for hours on end. I want to get better at making things people actually want though, so I'm trying to get more into the business and soft skill areas. I'm super excited to be in TAMID, and I can't wait to get to know everyone!

name: Garrett Button
intro: Hi everyone! I'm Garrett Button and I'm from Los Angeles! I'm a second year studying finance with a data science minor. I was in Boston last year but wish I was abroad as I love to travel. I'm excited at the chance to study abroad this summer or do a global co-op. Two of my biggest passions are photography and surfing. I love shooting sports and nature but will try anything! I've shot USC basketball, professional surfers and lots of national parks. I love the athletes and getting to see their excitement when they see the photos. I've met many cool people through this and seen awesome games while sitting on the court! I started surfing because it was the culture of my hometown but now I really love it. It's very relaxing and a great way to stay in shape. I definitely miss it while in Boston but it's an acceptable trade off. My goal is to grow old in a surf community where I can chill with my family and surf. I love watching surfers with a classic style like Mikey February and Mason Ho. One of the most fun parts of surfing is teaching friends so if any of you are ever in LA, let me know and we'll go surf! Since everyone talked about it, I also love music but more specifically old jazz/blues. Some of my favorites are John Lee Hooker, Muddy Waters and Otis Redding. I'll listen to anything but I love how Jazz can be used as a hype way start to the day and a slow unwind at night. If anyone has any song suggestions I would love to hear them! Lastly, I love to travel! Trips far away are amazing but some of the smaller camping trips have turned into my favorite weekends. My most recent trip was camping in Jalama, which is a state park in Central California. It's a remote beach with the most beautiful hiking I've ever done! I am super excited for this semester in TAMID and look forward to meeting all of you!

name: Pradnya Balamurugan
intro: Hi everyone! I'm Pradnya (my nicknames Pradhu), and I'm a second year Computer Science major. I'm from Trumbull, CT, which is about an hour away from New York. I was in Boston my first-year, but am really interested in studying abroad to explore new cultures and travel. I've only ever been to one other country, India, so being able to experience another country would be amazing! In terms of TAMID, I plan to do TCF this semester and joining TC in the future. I grew up dancing, specifically an Indian classical dance called Bharatnatyam, so naturally, I love to dance. I joined a bollywood fusion dance team here at Northeastern, Rangila, to hold onto that passion away from home. Now I've learned so many styles beyond classical like hip-hop, bhangra, and kuthu, which has been such a great experience. Due to my involvement in dance, I didn't get to try many other sports, but I swam in middle school and ran cross country in high school, somehow getting past my aversion to running. There are many things I want to try, like snowboarding and ice skating, but honestly I doubt my abilities! Just like a lot of you mentioned, I also love listening to music. My most listened to genres have to be rap, R&B, and bollywood/kollywood, but I'm open to any recs! I've been listening to SZA, Kendrick, Frank Ocean, and Brent Faiyaz a lot recently. I've only ever been to one concert, Travis Scott, but am so eager to go to more!! Even though I haven't watched too many, my all time favorite shows are Gilmore Girls and Suits. I could definitely use some ideas on what to watch next- I have not been able to find a good show to binge in so long! When it comes to movies, although it's hard to pick, some of my favorites have to be The Parent Trap and Matilda. Tangled and Cars are definitely my comfort movies. Lastly, I love hanging out with my friends, whether that's out exploring Boston or staying in and watching a horror movie. We're all major foodies so cooking/baking or eating out is definitely our top favorite thing to do. I undoubtedly leave them to do the cooking, as I am still yet to learn. Living in an IV forced double without a kitchen is not helping my case! I'm so excited to be apart of TAMID and even more excited to meet everyone!!

name: Sofia Atsalis
intro: Hi! My name is Sofia, and I am from Cape Cod, MA. I'm a first year Business Admin major, concentrating in finance. I'm looking to add a DS minor as well. I grew up so close to Boston, so now that I'm in college here it feels like a full circle moment. When I was younger, I used to tell my parents I would never go to school in a city because I thought they were overwhelming and scary. Needless to say, Boston's grown on me over the years and now I'm happier than ever to be here. I was an athlete all throughout high school (primarily soccer, also basketball and lacrosse). Those days are over for me and I'm totally washed up now, but I've found a new love for running to stay active. You can often find me running on one of the treadmills facing the street on the second floor of Marino blasting British club music through my Airpods. House music makes me run like the wind. Other hobbies include hanging out with my friends, playing electric guitar, reading, and creative writing. I don't play guitar as often as I used to, but I am always open to song suggestions. I can play pretty much anything. I usually read two books at a time: one fiction and one nonfiction so that way I never get bored. I really like writing short stories. Last Spring, every Tuesday night I would read my two best friends that lived down the hall from me bedtime stories that I wrote. Highlight of the week. I feel like I've covered a lot of ground on fun facts, so I'm going to pivot to future aspirations. I want to be able to bench at least one plate at some point in my life just so I can know what it feels like. When I'm older, I want enough room in my apartment for a giant DJ board in front of my sky view window. My final, and most realistic future aspiration is to ace my financial accounting exam tomorrow morning. Thank you for reading. I'm genuinely really psyched about TAMID. So excited to meet you all!

name: Dharmika Pendyam
intro: hey everyone! my name is dharmika, and i'm a first year who's undecided but i'm pursuing a data science & business administration major. i'm currently in the process of transferring from the explore program into d'amore mckim, so i'm super excited about that! i'm from a small town in jersey called chesterfield, which is about 20 minutes from princeton. i was actually born in boston, and during my entire college application process i was super adamant about only coming to a school here. when i committed to northeastern everyone called it a full circle moment. i'm not yet sure which track i'm interested in at TAMID (i'm indecisive if you can't tell), but i'm super excited to explore them all. i love all things fashion, cooking, and traveling. i find myself on newbury street at least once every two weeks, so if anyone wants to go on a spontaneous shopping trip i'd be so down! i've traveled to 17 countries and my favorite spots are peru, alaska, scotland, cabo, and paris. in my free time i loveee binge watching shows, i'm currently watching only murders in the building and waiting for season 5 of stranger things to come out. i need music to be playing when i'm doing any sort of work; some of my favorite artists are billie eilish, lana del rey, kendrick lamar, radiohead, and the neighbourhood. some of my bucket list items include volunteering in alaska's iditarod (my trip there this summer made me fall in love with the state) and backpacking across europe. back home, i have a younger sister who's basically a mini me and a havanese dog who acts high and mighty until he's approached by anyone. i'm sooo insanely excited to be a part of TAMID, and i can't wait to get to know all of you! please feel free to reach out if you ever want to hang :)

name: Harrison Schreiman
intro: Hi guys! My name is Harrison, but you can call me Harry. I'm a first year majoring in International Business with a concentration in Management. I'm thinking of adding a minor like Japanese or consulting, but I'm not sure yet. I'm from Orange County California, so this winter is definitely gonna be a change for me… I've been struggling without In-N-Out recently. Growing up, I played a lot of different sports. In middle and high school, I enjoyed watching volleyball and really liked playing it, but I never really got the hang of it (my coach neglected to teach rotations!). I've always been into weight lifting and bodybuilding. I first went to the gym in probably 7th grade. There's been a lot of breaks I've had, but I'm proud to say I still keep it up. I also did track and field in high school, and miss my jumps team very much. I did long and triple jump in specific. My shins were destroyed but it was definitely a lot of fun. I also love fantasy books. Message me about anything you're reading! I love to talk to people about books. Right now, I'm on the 4th book of the Red Rising series, and it's by far my favorite series ever. I also really enjoy The Beginning After The End, and plan to read 4th Wing next. By far my favorite hobby, and future aspiration, is to travel. I adore seeing new countries and traveling within them. This summer, I traveled to Scotland, England, the Netherlands, Germany, and Austria. I'm very excited to say I'll be going to Canada during Spring Break to help build houses for a local non-profit. I also love to explore Boston, and have a giant list of new food places to try or entertainment places to go (I really want to go to the F1 arcade or puttshack soon, also those square croissants by North End look amazing), so reach out if you wanna join. Around campus, I'm involved with the Alliance of Civically Engaged Students, and volunteer with Squashbuster's Tutoring Program. I've always enjoyed volunteering, and wanted to continue in college. If anyone needs more members for a cause or organization, totally reach out to me. I have some experience with building up non-profits, and would love to learn more. I also work at Marino, so come say hi if you ever seem me! I'm ecstatic to be a part of TAMID!! I can't wait to meet you all, and would love to go explore around Boston with y'all :)

name: Akhil Vallabhajosula
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Akhil, and I am a first-year majoring in Business & Law with a concentration in Finance and planning to do pre-law. I just finished up the nuin program and spent the semester in Berlin, Germany, so I'm definitely looking for suggestions for things to do in Boston. I'm from a small town in Connecticut called Wilton, which is about 45 minutes away from NYC. I am a big tennis fan and have played and watched since I was 5 years old and played in high school on the varsity team as well. I am also on the club tennis team at NEU. Throughout high school one of my biggest hobbies was to drum. I usually played sets and did some drum-line work as well. Fun fact about me was I got to be a part of a drumming show at MetLife Stadium. I'm also still trying to get back into soccer as I quit when I was younger (I wasn't very good though). I've also started to enjoy traveling as well and did a lot of it during nuin. Some bucket places I want to visit are Morocco, Spain, Slovenia, and Australia. Some of my favorite places I've already been to are Dubai, Prague, and Jamaica. I'm a HUGE music fan as well. If you see me walking around campus the chances are I have my headphones on playing something. Some of my favorite artists are Weston Estate, The Neighbourhood, The Weeknd, Bad Bunny, and Frank Ocean (even though he still hasn't dropped). I usually just listen to RnB or hip hop, but I love listening to Spanish music, house, and of course some nostalgic Coldplay songs from time to time. Definitely reach out if you have any song suggestions! Another hobby that I have is watching movies or TV series. Some of my favorites are the OG Lion King, The Pursuit of Happyness, and Whiplash. As far as TV shows, Suits is always a favorite along with Breaking Bad. (Looking for new suggestions because Netflix has been pretty dry recently). As far as other organizations around campus, I'm a part of the club tennis team and also in the pre-law frat Phi Alpha Delta. Not sure about this semester, but I also want to play some intramural sports maybe in future years. Super excited to be a part of TAMID, and can't wait to meet everyone!

name: Saariya Faraz
intro: Hi guys! I'm Saariya Faraz, and I am from Indore, India (central india). I am a sophomore majoring in Finance and Data Science. I was an NUin freshman, and I was sent to Mills in Oakland, CA...and it was a one of a kind adventure: hearing gun shots before going to bed and watching choppers chase people down on a regular night stroll to name a few. Being in a secluded, small campus did have its perks too..it was a tight knit community bonding over interesting experiences and the bay area food was top notch. For the most part of my life, I was very low maintenance in terms of food. I could only sense less salt and had pretty much no taste. However, living in an apartment, has turned me into a foodie and a cook, and I'm loving it! I'm a big fan of quirky/mind-bending content. The Boys, End of the f-ing world, Midsomer, Black Mirror, and Cocaine Bear are some examples. The Boys has to be the winner though, I don't think I have watched a better show. Some fun facts about me are that I can imitate pigeons, I had 2 imaginary best friends when I was three (I promise I had a happy, non-lonely childhood), and I broke my front tooth during a karaoke sesh (and believe it or not, that got me into college!). I enjoy going to the gym and trying out random yoga poses, and I could talk about stoicism for hours at a stretch. I'm very excited to be joining kuf class for spring '24, and can't wait to get to know everyone at TAMID!

name: Jayden Patel
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Jayden Patel, and I'm from Scranton, Pennsylvania, but I just moved to Westchester, New York over the summer. I am a first year, and I was in Boston first semester. My major is Business Administration with a concentration in Business Analytics. I hope to join the consulting track, but also try investment fund or tech consulting in the future. I'm basically addicted to watching sports. I love watching Football and Basketball, but I really love watching and playing Soccer. I like the Sixers, Phillies, Packers, and Dortmund, and I love following/watching specific players. I'm also obsessed with music. My taste is everywhere, but I especially love hip hop from the 90s, as well the early 2000's. I like Kendrick, Wayne, Nas, Blxst and like a hundred more artists. A fun fact about me is that I have an 11 hour 90's hip hop playlist so let me know if you want that! Some of my other hobbies are going to the gym, hanging out with friends, and watching movies or shows. My favorite show is probably Peaky Blinders. I also don't dislike any food, and I'll eat anything so I rarely have a preference. I'm really excited to meet and get to know everyone in TAMID so please reach out!

name: Alissa Rogozin
intro: Hey everyone! I'm Alissa and I'm from Brookline (about 20 min outside of Boston). I'm a first year computer science & design major and am interested in the TCF track, although this semester I won't be able to join bc of a class. I just got back from NUin in Madrid and I looooved it so much! I could see myself living in Madrid at some point, it's an amazing city. I grew up dancing (mainly ballet but also other styles) so that took up a lot of my free time. I also love spending time with friends by trying new food spots (give me recs pls!), thrifting, and just exploring new areas. Even though I grew up in Boston I'm trying to push myself to find new spots! I'm currently in my criminal minds rewatching phase but I also love gilmore girls, gossip girl, and suits. My favorite thing to do is definitely traveling. Some of my favorite place I've been to have been Berlin, Amsterdam, and Copenhagen, but I have so many more bucket list places!!! In my free time I also love listening to music. My goal this year is to go to more concerts so if anyone knows any artists coming to Boston lmk and I'd be so down to go together!! I'm really excited to be a part of TAMID and meet everyone :)

name: Javi Ibarguen
intro: Hey everyone! I'm Javi and I'm from Edgewater, NJ (right across the Hudson River from Manhattan). I've spent basically my whole life in NJ, I was born in New Haven, CT but only lived there for a year before moving to Philly, where I lived for a year as well. I'm a first year studying economics and finance, and I was in Boston last semester (regretfully). I'm interested in joining the investment fund and taking IFC, investing has been something of interest to me since I was 12, and I most likely will try to pursue a career in investment banking. I have been slacking since I've been here, but I'm generally pretty interested in running or weightlifting so feel free to help me get back in the exercise regime if that's something you're interested in. I follow most sports, I'm a huge UFC fan (no clue how that interest started tbh) and I also love watching football, most would call me a fantasy football prodigy (I got second to last place for two years in a row). I played soccer all my life but have gradually stopped following it as much. I listen to a ton of music, literally, all genres just depending on what I'm doing whether it's walking, studying, running, etc. I've also been listening to a couple podcasts to stay up to date with news within the financial service industry, but if anyone has any general news podcasts they would recommend, let me know. The most random hobby I've picked up in college is probably watching animal documentaries, and a bucket list item of mine is going on an African expedition. My long-term life goal is to own an NBA team, hopefully, in about 55 years I'll be there. I'm very excited to be a part of TAMID and look forward to getting to know everyone.

name: Jack Angell
intro: Hey everyone! I'm Jack Angell and I'm a first-year studying CS + Business with a finance concentration, but I'll probably switch to something data science-related soon. I'm from a small town in Northern Jersey so it's been great actually having stuff to do, see, and explore here in the Boston area. I spent my first semester doing NUin Madrid and really enjoyed the difference in pace and culture. Love racket sports despite my lack of skill for them. I repped upper-JV tennis and squash all throughout high school and would like to continue playing here at Northeastern. I'm also managing an IM hockey team passionately. Aside from sports, I spend a lot of time reading, watching movies, and listening to music. Favorite book: Dharma Bums by Kerouac, favorite movie: No Country for Old Men. Always looking for new books/movies so let me know if you have any recommendations! For music, I listen to a lot of Vince Staples, JID, and Action Bronson, but also Lenny Kravitz, Hendrix, and the Black Keys. I also love hiking and traveling— this past summer I went to Grand Teton and Yellowstone and had the time of my life. Since, the dream's been to move out west, live somewhere near the mountains, and get a ranch. Anyways, super excited to be a part of TAMID and I can't wait to talk to you all!

name: Eric Bao
intro: Hi all! My name is Eric Bao and I'm from Andover, MA but I've lived in a bunch of different cities internationally. I'm a first year returning from NUin Berlin majoring in International Business with intentions of switching into Business Admin and Design next semester. I played hockey up until the end of high school and would love to watch the Rangers (hopefully) win a cup soon. Also, I've been playing jazz saxophone for almost the last 10 years. With that said, my music taste is very all over the place but I've been listening to a lot of Al Green, Sade, and The Marias recently. I rarely find myself watching any TV shows but some of my favorite movies are Chunking Express and Fallen Angels by Wong Kar Wai and The Grand Budapest Hotel. A couple of my favorite reads recently have been Candide by Voltaire and some of Kafka's short stories. I'm very excited to be a part of the Kuf Class and getting to know everyone else in TAMID!

name: Alexis Montante
intro: Hi guys! I'm Alexis Montante, it's nice to meet all of you! I've lived in Newark, Delaware for the majority of my life but I've also lived in Houston, Texas and Shanghai for a couple of years. I moved to Buffalo, NY last year though, which was definitely a downgrade. I'm a second year studying Business & Design with a concentration in Marketing. I've figure skated all of my life and I'm also on the team here at Northeastern! Besides that my family is very big on skiing so I've grown up skiing. I love to travel as well, I studied abroad in Berlin my first semester of college and I am constantly out in Boston trying to explore new places. In my free time I read a lot and I love thrifting/fashion. I'm a really big art fan as well so MFA visits are quite frequent. I learned how to sail in middle school so I started sailing on the Charles last semester which has been super fun, especially when I can convince my friends to come with me. I love food too and I am ALWAYS down to eat or try a new restaurant so if anyone is ever hungry let me know! I have a longgg notes on my phone of my Boston restaurant reviews and it needs some updating. Anyways, I'm really excited to meet all of you and get to know you all better!

name: Saisha Ketkar
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Saisha Ketkar, I'm from Mumbai, India. I am a second-year student, majoring in Data Science and Economics with a minor in Environmental Studies. I am currently trying to survive the wild weather shift from Mumbai to Boston, but I love it here, especially during fall. Some of my favorite things to do over the weekend are to go for a walk around Newbury, get coffee with my friends and explore new restaurants. I started doing Taekwondo when I was 4, and I have played a couple of other sports such as handball and throwball growing up. I love to travel to new countries and cities and want to visit at least 5 more countries by the time I am 25. Outside of school, I love to hang out with friends, explore new places, and go running. Join me if you're up for a run sometime! I love painting and art, and I am currently taking an 8 am painting class too this semester. While I don't have any siblings, I have a very cute dog named Sailee. Fun fact - my friends and I helped dye each other's hair last semester. My favorite movie as of right now is Scott Pilgrim vs. the World, and my favorite shows are Arrested Development, The Office, Black Mirror, and Bojack Horseman. My favorite music artists currently are Dominic Fike, Clairo, and Noah Kahan. I'm very excited to be a part of TAMID and look forward to getting to know everyone, so feel free to reach out!

name: Meggan Shvartsberg
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Meggan Shvartsberg, and I'm from the suburbs near Philadelphia, PA. I'm a first year computer science major, with a concentration in Artificial Intelligence and a minor in math. I'm looking to learn more about AI/ML and gain more experience with web dev. My biggest hobby is definitely going to the gym (my favorite way to destress and stay sane haha), but I also love reading and drawing/painting (although I haven't made art in a while). My favorite books will forever be the Percy Jackson series, as it was a big part of my childhood. I am also very big on traveling (I've been to over 10 countries), and some of my favorite countries that I've been to are Croatia, Italy, and Greece. I love hiking and exploring new places. I'm really excited because I will be going to Australia this summer for my dialogue! I'm also always listening to music. Some of my favorite artists are Billie Eilish, David Kushner, and SZA, and I've recently been getting into Noah Kahan, Benson Boone, and Chance Pena. Also, some of my favorite shows to watch are Friends (my ultimate comfort show), Gilmore Girls, Outerbanks, The Good Place, and All American. I can't wait to join the TAMID community and get to know all of you :))

name: Jacob Benesch
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Jacob and I'm a first year studying Data Science & Business. I'm from just outside Baltimore, Maryland - aka Charm City! My whole life, I've had an interest for cooking and even made it to the final round before TV of MasterChef Junior when I was 12. Because of this, I love to explore all different types of foods - some of my favorite spots in Boston are Season to Taste in Cambridge and Liuyishou in Chinatown! I'm also a huge fan of everything Maryland - from our foods (crabs, crab dip, crab pretzels, crab hot dogs, basically all things crab) to our sports teams - the Ravens and the Orioles. I'm also an avid music listener and have been into some house music lately - lots of Fred Again, Fisher, and John Summit. But, I also love a good Drake, Don Toliver, and Frank Ocean playlist. One of my life goals is to see as much of the world as possible, and because of that I've kind of developed a hidden talent at finding airline miles deals and now book all my extended family's travel for them! Another fun fact about me is that I earned my 1st degree black belt in MMA back in 2021. Also, I have a crazy love for all things pickles and actually have a full pickle set (bucket hat, shirt, pajama pants, and pool floatie…) I'm so excited to be apart of this amazing community, and can't wait to meet you all!

name: Skylar Li
intro: Hello everyone! My name is Skylar Li, and I am an Industrial Engineering major from the Los Angeles area (Pasadena). Last semester, I was in Dublin as part of Nu.in, and I traveled a bit around Europe. Athens was my favorite by FAR, and my inner Percy Jackson fan was screaming. I also loved Amsterdam and seeing/buying all the Miffy merch I saw. In my spare time, I sleep, find new recipes for my rice cooker, and try to crotchet. I love cafe hopping with friends, and I will gladly take matcha/coffee recs (so far, I have had Matcha Maiko and Pavement Coffee). I also love journaling and kdramas! Some of my favorites are Vincenzo and Weak Hero Class 1. I am currently watching season 4 of Singles Inferno. Some of my mainstream favorites are Gilmore Girls (team Logan), The Office, and Modern Family. I am a big indie and KR&B listener. Some of my favorite artists are Peach Pit, The Backseat Lovers, Wave to Earth, Daniel Caesar, and COLDE. I also listen to a decent amount of Mario Kart/Legend of Zelda BOTW for extreme studying. Recently, I've been hooked on the Huberman Lab podcast. I am a major neuroscience nerd and just listened to an episode on the science of emotions with a Northeastern professor! Beyond exploring the realms of neuroscience, Huberman has made me appreciate the art of breaking down high-level ideas to a broader audience. On campus, I am involved in two service-learning courses and am volunteering at The Phoenix and Beachmont Elementary School! I also have a kitchen so please send recipes my way!! I am beyond excited to be part of the TAMID community and I look forward to meeting you all!

name: Jack Carroll
intro: Hi everyone! I'm Jack, I'm a second year studying data science. I'm from a tiny town in Maine called Limerick, so going starting college with a semester in Greece was such an incredible experience. Highlights included cliff jumping in Malta, skiing in Zermatt, and the amazing friends I made. Boston has been amazing so far too, catch me buying a $7 coffee or at Red Sox game. I'm a super active person, and I really love to see the outdoors. Skiing is my favorite combination of these two, but I also love to hike and golf. I've always been big on sports, playing football and baseball throughout high school. I'll always be a pats and Sox fan, and I also love to watch F1. You can always catch me in squashbusters, or running along the Charles which is my new obsession. If anyone knows how to deal with shin splints or knee pain please hmu. I've been trying to get myself back into reading after dropping it for a while. I finished Great Expectations a couple months ago and I'm almost done with Plato's Republic. Some of my all time favorites are Ender's Game, Harry Potter, and Peak. My roommates and I got a poker set this semester and I've been having way too much fun taking their money. I'm not a huge tv or movie guy but recently I've been rewatching Breaking Bad with my roommates, and watching Invincible. You're more likely to catch me playing brawl stars on my phone than watching tv. Music wise rap was my first love, favorites are JID, Kendrick, Wayne, Denzel, and Joey. I've been mixing it up the past year though, shoutout Noah Kahan, Sabrina Carpenter, Steely Dan, and Billy Joel. I am so excited to be apart of TAMID. In DS I'm engrossed in the technical so much of the time, so I'm really looking forward to learning how to apply some of these skills in a real context. Can't wait to meet all of you!

name: Jia Yu
intro: Hiii everyone! My name is Jialin Yu, but I formally go by Jia! I am currently a first-year studying Political Science and Business Administration with concentrations in Accounting, Legal Studies, and Law! I'm from Quincy, Massachusetts so about 20 minutes out from Boston, but I was originally born in China and came to the U.S. when I was 9. If anyone ever wants to go on a joyride, LMK. I love showing out-of-state friends to this amazing coffee shop called Marylous :) In my free time, I loveee playing volleyball or just staying active, hot yoga or pilates is my best friend. I'm in the process of learning how to cook and I've been growing my own sourdough starter but it's not going too well! I also love concerts, my favorite I've been to so far has to be The Weekend or Drake. I actually got the weekend tickets for 50 dollars when he was on tour 4 years ago, but this year, it's 400 dollars, which is so upsetting, but I'm seeing Tyler the Creator this summer!! Recently, I've been watching a lot of Gossip Girls so my question to you guys is would you rather be Blair Waldorf or Serena Van Der Woodsen and or Dan Humphrey and Chuck Bass? I am beyond excited to be a part of TAMID and to meet everyone :))) I hope to gain new meaningful insights on the consulting industry and to invest in my future endeavors! Also, I am going to LSE this summer so pleaseee send food recommendations and cool spots I can visit.

name: Ethan Pinho
intro: Hello everyone. My name is Ethan Pinho and I am a first year. I am double majoring in Bioengineering and Finance at Northeastern in pursuit of entering life science consulting. I am part of Alpha Epsilon Pi and participate in intramural soccer with some of my brothers. I am from Union, NJ (the state with better bagels and pizza). I am a huge soccer fan. My favorite league is the Prem and my team is Manchester United. It's been a tough last few years for United fans as many Prem followers may know. I have been playing soccer since I was three years old. I enjoy lifting in my free time to stay healthy and active. It allows me to diminish my stress levels and keep my energy levels high during the day. Additionally, I love hanging out with friends. I am excited to get to know everyone in Tamid and expand my circle with more wonderful, like-minded individuals. Recently, I have been trying to get into golf. In the summer, I played my first course (you can probably guess how that went). I am excited to go home for spring break to enhance my skills at my local driving range. My favorite TV show is Suits. I think all the main characters are perfect for the plot of the show. In addition, the character developments make the finale heartwarming to experience. I am super excited to get to know everyone and be part of the Tamid family!

name: Varun Kolluri
intro: Hey everyone, my name is Varun and I'm a freshman at DMSB majoring in Business Administration with a concentration in Finance. I'm from Greenwich, CT, where I've lived for over 17 years. It's a homely place that brings about fond childhood memories. To all the New Yorkers, CT has the best pizza in the world (Sally's and Frank Pepe's speak for themselves) so sit back, relax, and enjoy the feeling of second place. Outside the classroom, I'm super into sports. Long-distance running, football, basketball, tennis, spike ball, pickleball, you name it I got it. I vibe with the outdoors, whether it's going on fall nature hikes in upstate CT or playing football in the snow before settling down on my couch, grabbing a nice cup of hot chocolate, and watching the refs bail the Chiefs out for the 15th game of the NFL season. When it comes to music, there are two undeniable truths, 2000s music is the best, and 50 Cent is the best rapper oat. But other than that, I enjoy listening to any kind of music, be it rock or R&B. Recently, I've been trying to get back into shape to run a half-marathon. I want to get some runs in by the Charles River. Over the weekends, I love heading over to Marino to hoop for a bit. I'm happy to play with anyone interested, but just know that like Lavar Ball, I neva lost one on one. I'm super pumped to be part of TAMID. The laughter, the cries, the joy, the sadness, I'm here for each and every one of you, and the sky is the limit for the great things we can do to make this organization the best that it can be!

name: Dylan Avdoo
intro: Hello everyone. My name is Dylan Avdoo, and I am a first-year student. I am majoring in Business Administration with a concentration in Finance, but I am looking to add something else (maybe Psychology or Data Science). I'm from Woodmere, New York, which is where I have lived my entire life. It's a small town on Long Island. For everyone out there who thinks they have the best bagel spot, they haven't had More Than A Bagel—objectively the best bagels you'll ever have. Outside of schoolwork, I am a very active person. I'm super into the gym but also play sports. I play for the Men's Club Volleyball Team here and occasionally roll (jiujitsu). I also love going on adventures, whether that means hiking, going for a run, or taking a walk and enjoying nature. I'm not a huge music guy, but I've been slowly getting more into it after I went to a Coldplay concert with some of my friends. I listen to pretty much anything, so slide some recommendations my way, and I'll make sure to give them a listen. Watching movies has to be one of my favorite things to do when winding down, and my favorite movie is Good Will Hunting (if you haven't watched it, that should be your top priority in life). I'm stoked to be a part of the TAMID community and meet everyone. I hope to learn a bunch of new things while also being able to connect with every single person in this family!

name: Angelina Tung
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Angelina Tung, and I am a first year psychology and economics major. I'm from Summit, NJ (right outside NYC) and loved spending time in NYC with my friends in high school. I knew I wanted to go to a city school, so I'm really excited to be in Boston at Northeastern! I was a part of the NU In Greece program last semester and definitely miss the warm weather, sunsets, and affordable gyros. I had never been to Europe prior to this, but I was so glad I got to go to Malta, Norway, Croatia, and Austria! In my free time, I enjoy eating, dancing (specifically ballet and jazz), and working out. I was really excited to join the #foodies slack and am always down to try new places - let me know if you have any recs or ever want to grab food! Specifically, I love chocolate cake for breakfast, boba, and carbonara pasta. I also love staying active by going to the gym, although Marino is CRAZY crowded. I love to dance and am looking forward to hopefully taking dance and pilates classes in Boston! For music, my fav artists are Renee Rapp, Gracie Abrams, Rihanna, and Kendrick Lamar. Fun fact, I usually listen to rap and heavy metal while doing my homework because it helps me lock in. I also really love reading and have read 55 books this past year! My favorite genres are suspense, romance, and memoirs. My favorite books are Purple Hibiscus, Harry Potter, Educated, and The Silent Patient. Please let me know any good book recs! I'm super excited to be a part of TAMID! I can't wait to meet everyone and look forward to all the amazing memories we will make!

name: Connor Karr
intro: Hey everyone! I'm Connor, and I am excited to be joining TAMID! I'm a second-year student studying Computer Science and Economics. I'm originally from the tiny town of New Canaan, CT. When I'm not buried in algo problem sets, you can probably find me playing squash on the Squash B Team (we're goated, I swear). If the weather's nice, I'll be skateboarding around, trying not to wipe out in front of strangers, and when my brother is free, I love to go up north and go skiing and snowboarding. I've been skiing since I was five, but recently, I decided to change things up and pick up snowboarding, which is turning out exactly how it sounds. Another thing about me is that I'm the designated photographer for my friends. Need a new profile pic? A professional headshot? An Instagram worthy post? I got you. My most memorable photography adventures include visiting NYC and wandering around Lisbon, Portugal (follow me @Connor Karr). Like a lot of people here, I have a dog named Lucy, and she is an Australian shepherd, and not to brag, she is an award-winning show dog. I also love to cook, and my signature dish is le chicken piccata. If you ever need a Michelin meal, I'm your guy. Speaking of food, I'm currently on a mission to try every sandwich at Wally's. My personal favorites being the Eggs-on-the-Beach and Game of Thrones, so far, but I'm open to suggestions. In terms of music, my taste is all over the place. Right now, I've been listening to Mac Miller, Smino, BigXThePlug, Kendrick, beabadoobee, Lil Yachty, and Black Country, New Road. If you've got any recommendations, send them my way and I will give them a listen. I'm also a huge movie enjoyer, and I've watched over 400 movies and have a never-ending watchlist. Some of my all time favorites are Memento, Fantastic Mr. Fox, Monty Python and the Holy Grail, and Hereditary (my family is a big horror fan). I'm really excited to be part of TAMID and get the chance to meet all of you!

name: Alvin Reji
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Alvin Reji, and I'm a second-year studying Mechanical Engineering with a minor in Aerospace engineering. I'm from New Hyde Park, NY which is a town in Long Island.
