It's insane how many people at this school are from Long Island even though we're all the way up in Boston. I actually finished my first year of college at SUNY Buffalo before transferring to Northeastern, and I'm glad I got to escape that frozen tundra to a slightly warmer Boston. Going from a college surrounded by acres of forests to a city was really a big change and I'm all for it! In my free time I like to be active or social. Recently some things I've started to do more often are rock climbing, going to the gym, and just exploring different parts of Boston. I'm still fairly new to Boston, so if you have any must-see places please let me know! Also I'll gladly take any food reccs if you have any! Especially if it doesn't break the bank too much! I'm definitely going to keep an eye on that foodies channel. Some clubs I'm in are AerospaceNU and ASME. Another thing I've been doing is some undergraduate research, and it's been so fun being in the lab and playing around with fancy machines. I haven't found time to watch that many tv shows this semester but some shows I really enjoyed were Breaking Bad, Better Caul Saul, Suits, and How I Met Your Mother. I've seen so many people talk about their favorite music, but its gonna be heartbreaking to hear that I don't really listen to music… but I'm willing to change that! If you have any artists or albums you enjoy, let me know! I'm so excited to be part of the TAMID community and I look forward to meeting you all!

name: Krisha Dani
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Krisha Dani, and I am a first-year studying Computer Science with a minor in Economics. I'm from Nashua, NH, which is roughly an hour north of Boston. Growing up in NH, I spent a lot of my time outdoors, so I really enjoy being outside and taking hikes, going skiing, bike rides and walks, and enjoying the fall colors in the autumn. In my free time, I love to play pickleball, go to the gym, paint and draw, read, and explore new places. I love to walk around the city and just take all of it in (especially when it's warmer). Trying new food and windowshopping in Boston is definitely an ideal day for me. I also love to cook and try new recipes. My favorite thing I made recently is a roasted tomato and garlic toast with pesto and mozzarella! I'm also experimenting with different pasta sauces, the last two I made was a vodka sauce that I incorporated tofu in for protein (you couldn't even tell!) and a lemon parmesan sauce. Lastly, I love to dance. I'm on New England Bhangra Club (NEBC) here at northeastern. Bhangra is a traditional dance originating from Punjab, India. I love going to comps and performing- this year we've gone to comps in North Carolina and Virginia, and we won crowd favorite at Northeasterns Dance4Me! In terms of entertainment, my favorite TV shows are I Love Lucy and Boy Meets World (ik super old), and I am a huge Tecca fan. I am excited to get to know all of you and be part of this amazing community!

name: Isha Mehta
intro: Hiiiii guys!!!! I'm Isha Mehta, I'm a first year studying Econ and Finance! I live 40 mins away here in the suburbs in a town called North Andover. My passions include doing absolutely anything with my friends, exploring new places to eat (huge foodie so lmk if you wanna try a new restaurant together), working out (I'm running a half marathon this year so if you wanna go on runs HMU), getting matcha/coffee/yummy drink, rotting in my bed, shopping, and traveling. I went to Thailand in December and it was the BEST TRIP EVER and a few other of my favorite places are London and Portugal. I'm going to Italy in May and am SO EXCITED. I also have a huge sweet tooth and am always down for anything that involves chocolate or ice cream. I've danced my whole life and did Bollywood dance and classical Indian dance. I also swam competitively all throughout middle and high school. I'm not an avid movie watcher so I haven't seen a lot of the classics, so if anyone has any recommendations that I need to watch pls lmk. My favorite shows are Suits, Jane the Virgin, Sex and the City, Gossip Girl, and The Good Place. I love love love music and am always listening to it. Some of my favorite artists are Don Toliver, Future, Drake, SZA, Brent Faiyaz, Daniel Caesar, and Frank ocean. Huge rap and R&B person but I also love house. I love Fred again, Dom Dolla, Fisher, and Mochakk! I'm so excited to be a part of TAMID and get closer with you guys, I can't wait for what's to come!!!!

name: Kashi Shah
intro: Hello everyone! My name is Kashi Shah and I'm a first year studying business and finance with a minor in design. I'm hoping to also add a minor in French because I'm actually fluent! I'm born and raised in the city in Chicago and went to a French school my entire life. I have a lot of international friends because of this and spent most of my summer with them in Europe, mainly in France. I live in Lincoln Park and absolutely love living in the city. If you ever need any recommendations lmk!! The best deep dish in Chicago is Pequod's Pizza. In my free time, I love to travel, stay active, go to new restaurants, and watch movies/tv shows. I played basketball, soccer, and volleyball in high school. I'm a huge tv show watcher; if there's a show you like I've probably seen it. My favorite show is New Girl and most recently I've been watching The OC. I've also been reading Greek mythology recently and am almost done with Cirse. I'm also super passionate about fashion, my goal is to do something with the intersection of business and luxury fashion in the future. I love to shop, curate outfits, and stay up to date in the world of design. My favorite foods are sushi, my grandmas Indian cooking, and tapas. I also love fro yo, chocolate cake, and strawberries and Nutella. Recently I've been listening to a lot of house and jazz music. I'm so excited to get to know everyone and be apart of TAMID :)

name: Helena Dube
intro: Hi everyone!! My name is Helena and i'm first year studying business and finance, but I am considering a minor in DS. I'm from the suburbs of Chicago, about 30 minutes away from the city! Some things that get me out of bed in the morning are music, coffee/matcha runs, running/pilates, and spending time with friends! I love listening to music and always love new recs (feel free to hmu), but I also love making music and learning instruments. I am interested in learning more about what's on campus for music (lmk!!) I got into fitness in high school, where I rowed all four years. After four years, I realized I enjoyed the cross training more than the actual sport, and developed a love for running and yoga. Im testing my love for running by doing a half next fall! Fun fact about me is that my mom is a core power and Pilates instructor so I get a free membership. (HMU for a run or class!!) I also love to travel, especially to Portugal to see my family!! I've been lucky to get to travel around the world and my one of favorite parts is getting to try new foods. Another fun fact is that I'm pescatarian. Seafood hits different in Portugal. But anywhere I am I'm a big foodie so I'm always down if anyone wants to try new places or recipes.

name: Michael Crosby
intro: Hello everybody!! My name is Michael Crosby, but most people just call me by my last name, Crosby. I am a second year studying Accounting. I am from Tenafly, NJ, which is around 30-40 minutes from NYC. Outside of the classroom, I am a pretty active guy. I play for the Men's Club Ice Hockey Team here and when I am not in school, I play competitive golf. I also love going to the gym to help keep me active. I have played these sports since I was a kid, so I have a deep love and appreciation for them. I am also trying to get into skiing, as my friends have been trying to convince me to get into it. However, it is difficult as hockey season has been quite busy! I am no Gordon Ramsay, but I love trying new and interesting food combinations and recipes to keep me fueled, so if you have any please let me know. At home, I have a younger sister in her freshman year of college and a cat, who we adopted from a shelter back during COVID. He is 13 pounds of personality and he is wonderful. Like a few other people here, I don't really listen to music. I get a lot of hate for this, and I feel it is very reasonable. I am trying to slowly get into music through reccomendations, so if you have any, please feel free to let me know! I started to enjoy traveling more since I first started college. I went to Punta Cana for spring break, and that was by far the most fun I had during a trip. Then, over this past summer, I visited my college friends and loved every single bit of it. This year, I am not yet sure where I will be traveling, but hopefully I can find out where soon! I am very excited to join the TAMID community and meet all of you! I hope to connect with everyone and make some wonderful memories!

name: Madhav Nair
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Madhav, and I am a second year majoring in computer science with a concentration in AI. I am from San Jose, CA (about an hour south of San Francisco). I spent my first year in Oakland. A lot of people who spent their first year in Oakland always talk about how bad it was, but coming from California, I absolutely loved it. It was such a unique experience that I grew a lot from. However, we are onto bigger and better things here in Boston. I am really big into sports. I have played basketball since I was in elementary school, and I would say it is my favorite sport to play and watch. Football is a close second. Niners and Warriors for life!! However, I wasn't good enough at either sport to play in high school, so I ran track instead. I fell in love with the sport, and I committed a lot of time into it in high school. I honestly find it hard to meet other people who have ran track, so if you have, please hit me up! If you catch me listening to music, I am probably listening to Travis Scott or Drake. I am pretty confident that I can name any Travis Scott song within the first 2 seconds of the song. I am almost there with Drake, but he just has too much music. I have been expanding my taste in the past year. I have started listening to a lot of Tory Lanez, Brent Faiyaz, and Bryson Tiller. I always thought I was a huge music fan until I met some of my friends who listened to music for over 100,000 minutes in a year. I don't know how they could possibly do that. The most I have ever had is around 40,000 minutes. There are some people who are listening to music 24/7, and I just am not at that level yet. I am more than thrilled to be a part of TAMID. I loved everyone I talked to during recruitment, and I am excited to get to know everyone else. My goal by the end of this semester is to know at least one fun fact about every member in TAMID.

name: Bhuvan Hospet
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Bhuvan and I'm from Monroe, CT. I'm a first year studying computer science/business but i'm considering switching into data science. I spent my first semester here in Boston, and so far I've really enjoyed it! In my free time, I enjoy listening to music – some of my favorites are Kanye, Outkast, and Kendrick (the halftime show was amazing imo). However, I'm always down to listen to new genres or artists, so let me know if you have any recommendations. This summer I played a lot of pickleball for fun, and even though I haven't played that much in college, I'm looking forward to the weather warming up. I also love watching sports, with football being my favorite, but basketball is definitely up there. As for TV shows, some of my favorites are Arrested Development, Breaking Bad, and Game of Thrones (i'm on my 3rd rewatch). I'm also surprised that so many people here have watched HIMYM, because I just finished watching it over break. I'm really excited to get to know everyone in TAMID, and to be a part of this semester's education class!

name: Ioanna Damianov
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Ioanna and I'm a first year studying cognitive psychology & computer science. I've lived in Princeton, New Jersey my whole life and will never get tired of wandering around the town and trying all the sweet treats. I'm a massive foodie and I will eat literally anything. I also love cooking, especially with other people. My world-renowned (aka my family, my dogs, and a handful of friends) dishes include Banitsa (a Bulgarian pastry), air-fried chimichangas, and seafood pasta! Last semester, I was in N.U.in Greece and I'm so grateful for my experiences there. I made incredible friends, ran a half-marathon, got to visit my family in Bulgaria, and traveled around Europe (Vienna was definitely my favorite trip). I love reading (follow me on goodreads!) and I've been trying to read 50 books a year, though not very successfully. Some of my favorites are Normal People, Demon Copperhead, and Daisy Jones & The Six and I'm always open to book recs (tbr can never be too long). I will also occasionally indulge in some writing, though it's never very good. Staying active always keeps me going. I like lifting, going for walks, doing yoga, pilates, and running. If anyone is looking for a running buddy let me know! The goal is to one day run a marathon so maybe we can work up to it together! I'm super excited to be part of the TAMID community and I can't wait to get to know everybody! My favorite thing to do is exploring new places and would love to get to know Boston better if anyone has cool recommendations for things to do or wants someone to go somewhere with!

name: Susanna Kramer
intro: Hi guys!! My name is Susanna and I'm a first year studying international business and finance with a minor in French. I am from Portland, Oregon and spent my first semester in NUIN Germany. This was such a cool experience because I got to experience a completely different culture for my first semester of college and I loved getting to travel around Europe. At Northeastern, I am currently involved in TAMID, Women in Finance and Global Research Consulting. Outside of school, I love to read, go to the gym and you'll likely see me walking around campus with a coffee in my hand. In high school I played golf and when I'm home I like spending time outside when the weather allows for it. I love listening to almost all types of music and getting to know other people's music tastes so I can discover new songs. My favorite foods are pasta, a really good salad, and smoothies. I also have a huge sweet tooth so if you ever want to go get a sweet treat I am probably available. I am so excited to get to know you all and for this semester and years to come!!

name: Jessica Poprik
intro: Hey everyone!! My name is Jess, and I'm a first-year Finance major! I'm also thinking about adding a minor or maybe even a double major in Data Science (fingers crossed my DS classes go well!). I'm from Robbinsville, New Jersey! I'm a HUGE music lover and actually ended up in the top 5% of Spotify listeners last year. I literally listen to everything, so I'd love any song recommendations you've got! Right now, I'm really into The Weeknd, Frank Ocean, Billie Eilish (Hit Me Hard and Soft is on repeat!), Tate Mcrae, Gracie Abrams, and Arctic Monkeys. I've also been playing the violin for 14 years, so classical music is my go-to when I need to focus. One of my favorite things to do (that most people probably don't know about me) is rock climb. I've been climbing since I was nine, and it's such a fun way to let off steam. I'm also a big fan of the gym or just strolling around Boston to stay active. Traveling is one of my biggest passions! When I was 15, I convinced my parents to let me study abroad in Barcelona for 4 weeks (it was AMAZING!). Since then, I've had the chance to visit 8 countries through NUin, and some of my favorite spots have been London, Budapest, and Paris. But Prague will always have my heart—I studied there and highly recommend it, especially during Christmas when the markets are magical! I also love cooking and baking, but sadly, there's no kitchen in my IV forced double :(. If anyone has a kitchen and wants free food, hit me up! I'm always down to try new recipes. A few fun extras about me: I have a twin sister studying at WashU in St. Louis! I'm a full-on caffeine addict—you'll never see me without coffee or a Celsius in hand! I'm always looking for new shows and movies to watch, so send me your faves! I'm so excited to be part of this amazing community, and I can't wait to see what the future has in store!

name: Rishi Dilip
intro: A little tardy to the introductions but as they say better late than never. My name is Rishi. I am from a suburb 20 minutes out from Seattle. I am currently a first-year majoring in Computer Science and Business. Within TAMID, I am enrolled in TCF and looking to go down the TC path in the future. I am a huge sports lover. Growing up, I couldn't stick to a sport, which meant I was a jack of all trades but, unfortunately, master of none. So I have played cricket, soccer, flag football, basketball, and finally, tennis, for which I played doubles on my high school varsity team. I am also a very passionate fan of many of those sports, most notably football, where I am a diehard Seahawks fan (As many of you can imagine, now living in Boston, I am constantly reminded about the 1-yard line of a certain Superbowl). I am also a big-time car enthusiast, so much so that my father brings me along every time he purchases a new car because I usually know more about the car in question than the salesman. In the summer of junior year, my friend and I took apart his BMW e92 335i. As part of the process, we wrapped the car, installed a new exhaust, replaced the intake, and tuned the car. To this day, it is one of my favorite projects that I worked on. Now, I also love driving cars; I am fortunate enough to have taken some of my dad's cars on the track, as well as a few supercars over the years. I love to travel. Some of my favorite places I have visited are Switzerland, specifically Lucerne and Interlaken, New Zealand (South Island), and Italy. Still on my list is: Africa, Iceland, and Greece. In terms of Music in recent years, my taste has diversified. On the hip-hop side, I am a big Travis Scott fan, and I was lucky to get floor tickets for Utopia. Additionally, I like Drake, Don Toliver, 21, and Ye (only his music). On the R&B front, I love Tory, PND, Bryson Tiller, and Brent Faiyaz. Additionally, there is a bit of old 80s rock in Bryan Adams and Richard Marx. Growing up in the pop era, I have a throwback playlist with old hits from Charlie Puth, Ed Sheeran, Bruno Mars, The Weeknd, One Direction, Maroon 5, etc. My roommate has even tried to convert me down the country path, but needless to say, it is still a work in progress. It's been great to meet many of you over the past few weeks and I can't wait to get to know everyone through my time at TAMID.

name: Brianna Quinn
intro: Hey guys! I'm Brianna, and I'm a second-year data science and business administration major (with a concentration in fintech!) This semester I'm taking TCF & IFC, and i'm taking ORGB. I was born in China but raised in the greater Philly area (GO BIRDS). I've been a member of the NU Jazz Ensemble for 4 semesters and counting! I play alto saxophone for the band, but i also play 4 other instruments: tenor sax, clarinet, ukulele, and guitar. I also enjoy singing (fun fact: I used to be in an a capella group in hs and we have a few albums available for streaming). I am also an amateur music theory enjoyer lol I'm also a member of this year's NUGOAL cohort—Northeastern University's Growth and Opportunities for Asian Leaders. Some other hobbies of mine include working out (second degree black belt in MMA!), powerlifting, gaming, photography, and journaling. On the topic of lifting, I feel like lately I've been putting off gymming, so if anyone would like a gym buddy, lmk!!! I also just love trying new things, at the beginning of last semester I was learning how to play tennis with my friends and I also joined my roomie's IM volleyball team (despite having no prior experience). My loves include: my dog, a poodle-terrier mix named Ray; going out to eat; concerts (2x Boygenius, 2x Beabadoobee, 1x Conan Gray, 1x Wallows, + some other more niche artists—it's not much, but I am trying); purchasing sweet treats and overpriced drinks, traveling & sightseeing (i went to Copenhagen last spring break!); fruit, especially sliced; indie-alt-rock music; all types of skating; trader joe's (as a former employee, i can get u a discount); collecting trinkets & blind boxes; 0.5 flicks; impulsively chopping my hair; digital cameras; cassette tapes; Spotify (Apple Music thumbs down); shaved ice; stuffed animals; grandpa sweaters and baggy clothing; noise cancellation headphones; and studio ghibli films. As for professional background, I'm currently on co-op at Penn Mutual as a Financial Manager (though I'm not actually doing much finance). And this past summer, I was a Digital Product Dev intern for Insomnia Cookies! So excited to meet everyone & so thankful to be here!!!

name: Valentina Benitez
intro: Hiii guys!!! I'm Valentina (Val). I'm a second-year studying Business and Psychology with a concentration in Marketing, and I'm thinking about adding a Data Science minor. I currently live in Cincinnati, but I've moved around a ton. I'm originally Venezuelan and I've also lived in Brazil, Switzerland, and Panama. In my free time I LOVE watching psychological thrillers, trying new restaurants, and running. I'm actually training for a half marathon with my roomates this November! I'm also obsessed with matcha, so I learned to make it at home and it's one of my current fixations (LMK if you want to try it). I'm a big traveler and love hearing about other people's experiences all over the globe. I'm going to Portugal for winter break for the first time and cannot wait! In high school I played soccer competitively and ran track & field (relays + long distance). A fun fact about me is that over the summer I work at Tropical Smoothie, so I have a ton of their secret recipes to make yummy smoothies. I speak Spanish, English, and Portuguese (happy to switch languages anytime!). I also have a very serious Sporcle habit—specifically the "countries of the world" quiz—and I like to think I'm the best at guessing every country. In terms of music, I honestly like all genres but the best concert I've been to was definitely Bad Bunny. I'm so incredibly excited to be part of TAMID and meet all of you soon!!

name: Dang Nguyen
intro: Hii everyone!! I'm Dang, and I'm a second-year studying Data Science and Math. I grew up in Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon), Vietnam, and moved to Boston last year for college. In my free time, I love going on morning runs. I've been able to run 3 miles almost every day for the past month. I'm also a big Vietnamese food enthusiast. A fun fact is that pho, which is so popular abroad, is actually one of the more overlooked dishes in Vietnam since we have so many other regional specialties that people eat more often, such as com tam (broken rice with grilled pork chop) or bun bo Hue (Hue-style beef noodle soup). Being far from home, I've been trying to cook for myself, though I haven't found as much free time as I'd like. Another hobby of mine is playing pool. Whenever I go back to Vietnam, I always play with my friends and sometimes compete in local tournaments to improve my skills. This past summer, I even decided to buy my own pool cue. I also really enjoy playing soccer, and I've heard a lot of people in TAMID play too, so I'm excited to hopefully join in some games! I'm so excited to be part of TAMID and can't wait to get to know all of you better!

name: Clement Baba
intro: Hi everyone! I'm Clement, a 2nd-year Computer Science and Business Administration major, concentrating in Finance. I spent my first year in Oakland, and then in London, and it was great. I was born in Nigeria, and I live in Maryland. I'm looking forward to learning about the different tracks, and I'm particularly interested in the Investment Fund. If there's something you should know about me, it's that I like to be everywhere. I like sports, music, travel, tech, finance, food, and I'm always down to learn something new. I particularly like playing basketball, and I've recently been trying to get more consistent at golfing (I'll be better than Tiger by the end of the year, trust me). I really like to travel, and by the end of this year, I'll have visited 8 countries just in 2025. I hope to visit every country at some point, but some places have been so great that I'll probably just frequent them. I'm excited to go to Dubai and Nigeria this winter break. I'm Nigerian, and the first time I ever went on a plane was to Dubai, so lots of nostalgia this winter for me. Some other things I'm interested in are brain teasers and puzzles. I played the NY Times games religiously every day, till they made the mini cost money. I'm a bit of a big back, and I'll put you guys on. Get the George likes his chicken spicy at Wally's on a long ciabatta roll with extra guac and mayo. I played football, basketball, and ran track in high school, but quit track after one year (too many sweats). This summer, I interned in DC on Capitol Hill; It was 25 minutes from my house with no traffic, but it took me an hour and a half to get there every day. I was home most of the time during the summer, but I did get to go to a Davido concert in Philly. I love Afrobeats, and I'm always happy to share playlists and talk music. I'm really looking forward to a great community here at Tamid, and excited to get to know you all more!

name: Rishi Koduri
intro: Hey everyone!!! My name is Rishi Koduri, yes the "Crunchy Roll" guy. I don't even watch anime like that. I'm a second year majoring in Cybersecurity and Business Admin concentration in finance. I'm from the city with the greatest sports teams and food to exist, HOUSTON TEXAS!! But I still love Boston more. All my friends know this about me and I wanted to share it with y'all too. I love to talk during movies, I hate watching movies where everyone is just silent. My favorite movie of all TIME is Baby Sitter 1 & 2. The movie is utter garbage but the experience you have watching it with friends is amazing. It is an amazing movie to watch while talking and laughing with friends. I love to Travel, I went to Japan this summer with my 7 high school friends which was bound for disaster. We are all very... "energetic", so we had a lot of interesting moments. It was still a very cool experience to have with my home town friends and had a blast. To add on to that PLEASE let me know if you want to explore Boston outside of downtown, I unfortunately did not do much of that last year. My music taste is very… um… interesting! I love to listen to Disney music, from old movies like Kung Fu Fighting, Your Welcome, etc. I also tend to find a new artist time to time and listen to there albums so much to the point in which I end up getting sick of them. My current victim is LUCKI, he's music is tuff for now. I also love listening to Japanese Car music, it has an interesting vibe to it. I also have a playlist for a certain time in the day, Gym, studying, or just walking around. I also have a few bucket list items I thought to share incase some one shared a similar interest to me: Go on a scary roller coaster (I am TERRIFIED of roller coasters), Retrieve my old Roblox account from 2016, Hide n Seek in IKEA with a lot of people. Outside of TAMID, I just joined Northeastern's Taekwondo team. I have 0 years of experience, but I watched Cobra Kai last year and I am interested in high kicking people. I am also on the E-board for Northeastern's Capture The Flag Club. No its not the capture the flag your thinking of, I am referring to cybersecurity capture the flag, which have challenges you have to solve. I'm so exited to be in TAMID this semester, and can't wait for the journey.

name: Braden Benzan
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Braden and I'm a second year majoring in Business Admin with a concentration in Finance and minor in Data Science. I grew up just south of Boston in Milton, MA and have remained in and around this amazing city for most of my life. Luckily for me, I started my time at Northeastern as a part of the Nu.in program in Madrid where I got to learn a lot about Spanish culture as well as travel all around Europe (really my first time traveling). My favorite trips of that semester were by far Zermatt (Switzerland) and Chamonix (France) for some insane skiing, as well exploring Barcelona for a 5-day Halloweekend. I also got the chance to visit a few other places so if anyone's looking for recs around Spain or Europe definitely let me know! I also got the chance to play Soccer for the SLU varsity team and compete against other universities around the city. Experiencing Spanish soccer culture firsthand is an unreal feeling, and watching Real Madrid defeat FC Barcelona in that fall's El Classico was a highlight of the semester. Now back in the US, I spend a lot of my free time involved in some sort of physical activity. Here at Northeastern, I'm on the Club Baseball team, playing 2 intramural sports (Flag football, and ofc Soccer), Weightlifting, as well as Personal Training for clients at the Marino Center. I find a lot of these activities help me unwind and I get to enjoy the last of the summer weather before it slips away in a few more weeks. Aside from this, I recently got into golfing (still have a horrible slice from a 15 years of baseball) as well as pickleball, and I love cooking, running, and SKIING (only thing that gets me through the Boston winters). For entertainment, I (tw) don't like much TV and have serious commitment issues when I comes to completing a full show. Unfortunately this means I will probably never finish any show you mention I "have to watch" (Sorry!). I love love music and will listen to everything under the sun (minus country), but my favorite genres right now are House and R&B (Anyone going to Sammy Virji next month?). I'm working on going to more concerts (only been to 2 in my life) so if anyone's got some good recs I'm all ears! Getting more personal, I have a great family from all over the world including Venezuela, Ecuador, Spain, Egypt, and the Dominican Republic and despite the heavy Latin roots and time abroad in Spain, I have very questionable Spanish skills (I'm working on it!). I was instead placed in a French program throughout school and while I still consider myself fluent I'm definitely a little rusty, so if anyone else is looking to practice please let me know! Super excited to be a part of TAMID this semester and I'm looking forward to getting to know everyone!

name: Hallie Luo
intro: Hi everyone!!! I'm Hallie and I'm a second-year Business Admin major, concentrating in marketing and minoring in psychology! I'm from Wayland, Massachusetts (30 minutes outside of Boston). I was in N.U. In Rome my first semester which was a ton of fun, but pretty much all I learned was Ciao and my loving connection to pasta. I think the best pasta shape is rotini but feel free to debate that. A few random fun facts/things about me: I love clothes/fashion & thrifting — I work at Free People Movement on Newbury almost strictly for the employee discount (hmu if you want 40% off at FP or Urban Outfitters). I LOVE music and making Spotify playlists. Going to concerts is like my favorite activity ever (I went to 8 this summer). I love everything but probably listen to indie rock/folk the most. I also love road trips, going to the gym, photography, cooking/baking, watching tv (currently on S3 of Scandal), and I'm also a big foodie. I love NY Times Puzzles and am a frequent Spelling Bee + Crossword enjoyer. This paragraph is really all over the place but I'm also a Celtics fan. Currently level 10926 in Candy Crush, pretty mediocre at but enjoy chess, somehow level 38 in Pokemon Go from the COVID days, and miss playing Minecraft. I promise I'm like normal though. I also just joined Alpha Chi Omega on campus! Looking forward to meeting everyone and being a part of TAMID!

name: Vibhu Gangina
intro: Hey everyone. My name is Vibhu and I was born in Middletown, Connecticut and lived there for ~8 years, but I now reside in Avon, Connecticut. I also love eating food and plan to check out the sleeper spots in Boston. This summer, I went a bunch of places. I went to San Diego (on a senior trip), Nevada, and Banff National Park. I got into photography a couple months prior to the summer, so I brought my cheap DSLR and took a lot of pics at these places. I also worked an internship at "The Hartford," an insurance company...in Hartford, Connecticut. I'm so excited to be part of TAMID and can't wait to meet all of you soon!

name: Kevin Yi
intro: What's up, good people. I am Kevin, and I am a second-year majoring in Political Science and Economics with a minor in Data Science. I spent the first 10 years of my life in Hangzhou, China (beautiful city, lots of lakes and mountains) before moving to Lakeville, Connecticut (middle of nowhere) to go to boarding MIDDLE SCHOOL. Since then, my family has also moved to the U.S., and we now live in New Haven, CT (home to the best pizza in the world, not even playing). I am hoping to be a part of the Investment Fund track in TAMID, although I'm still open to other tracks. I started my time at Northeastern in London, where I spent two semesters learning, goofing around with friends, backpacking 6 countries in Europe (make sure you check hostel reviews before you book them), and just had a good time immersing myself in the British culture. I definitely want to go back at some point. My first trip to Iceland with my friends was my favorite one. We rented a car and drove around the ring road, saw a lot of nature, and even made our own hot spring and cooked lamb with the geothermal energy from volcanic rocks. With hobbies, I am all over the place. I enjoyed nature and the outdoors. I have been an avid hiker for a while now and have hiked 19 out of 33 Catskills peaks above 3500 ft. I enjoy basketball (Steph Curry GOAT), music, traveling, singing, arts and crafts (although I'm not very good at it), gaming, cooking, skating, and just trying out new things. I played basketball for three years in high school before tearing my ACL while skating down a massive hill in my senior season. But I am bouncing back and am happy to see all the hoopers in TAMID. My top five artists at the moment are Giveon, Daniel Caesar, Lauryn Hill, Mac Miller, and Drake (Of course). For TV shows, I am locked in on Ozark and rewatching How I Met Your Mother at the moment. Outside of all that, I have a little brother who just started his first year in college, a little sister who just started 7th grade, and an absolutely adorable Shiba Inu (her name's Yuki). Can't wait to meet everyone in TAMID, and let's get cooking TAV class.

name: Alex Foroohar
intro: What's up everyone! I'm Alex, a 2nd year Finance and Economics student. I'm from Brooklyn, NY but was born in London. Within TAMID, I hope to go into the Investment Fund Track after IFC and also try to join the Social Engagement committee as well. More importantly, I'm really excited to be an avid member of brainrot committee. I'm super big into sports (love soccer and basketball but follow pretty much all of them.) I'm super big into traveling and am definitely going to try studying abroad in Europe next year. Really looking forward to getting to know everyone else in TAMID and having a great rest of the semester!

name: Otto Cline
intro: Hey guys! My name is Otto and I am a second year CS and Business major from Queens, NYC. I'm really big on software development and am planning on going into the tech consulting track. If I didn't make it clear with that first paragraph, I'm also a massive CS nerd so hit me up if you're going to a hackathon and looking for a team member. Really excited for TAMID and can't wait to get to know you all!

name: Sankalp Mundra

All-time favorites: I Want It That Way by the Backstreet Boys, anything George Michael, anything Elvis, anything Bublé, you get the idea… I can proudly say that I am the best golfer here. name: Ydalia Rodriguez
intro: HI EVERYONE!!! I'm Ydalia Rodriguez and I'm a first year studying data science and business administration. I'm from Fort Lee, NJ, but I grew up in Brooklyn, NY. name: Kyle Shibata
intro: Hey! My name is Kyle Shibata and I'm a second year Computer Science and Business(Finance) student from Pasadena CA, interested in the Tech Consulting track. Some of my favorite things to do are playing soccer, playing the ukulele, snowboarding, hiking, trying new foods, and maximizing shareholder value. I recently went on a quick road trip to New Hampshire and Vermont which was a load of fun. Let me know if you have any food or travel bucket list items you're down to scratch off with me or any must try recommendations. Can't wait to meet y'all!

name: Allie Bienaime
intro: Hi everyone!!! I'm Allie and I'm a second year. I'm from the Bay Area and grew up splitting time between San Francisco and Palo Alto. I'm an International Business and Economics major with a concentration in Finance and I spent my first semester at NUin Lisbon. HEAVY emphasis on matcha. At Northeastern, I'm involved in WeInvest, NUEcon Press, NU Downhillers, NUDANCO, and Sigma Delta Tau. Some facts about me: I went to Burning Man when I was fourteen, I've memorized tying twelve different sailing knots, I MUST end every meal with a pickle or at least some sort of savory element, I shamelessly competed in chariot racing at Classical Latin conventions in Middle School, I don't charge my phone at night because I don't own an extension cord, I have never had a Wollie's sandwich (I do intend on trying one very soon so I'm curious as to which are your favorites). I am so so so excited to be a part of this amazing community and I can't wait to meet you all!!!

name: Isabel Banks
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Isabel Banks, I am a second year studying Industrial Engineering and Business Administration with a concentration in Finance. I am also minoring in math. name: Pranav Agarwal

name: Aman Desai
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Aman Desai, and I'm from Poconos, Pennsylvania. I'm currently a second-year studying Industrial Engineering and Business Administration, with a concentration in Finance on the consulting track. For my freshman year, I was in NUin Greece, where I had the opportunity to travel to many countries. Happy to be back on the Boston Campus this year — excited to explore more. My favourite hobbies include cooking and baking. I started when I was around 6 or 7 years old and have been experimenting with more difficult recipes since. I just got on Beli — if y'all got any W food recs send them my way! Music has been a huge part of my life. I started playing the piano when I was 6/7 years old, and competed for a minute. (I was that one kid in gym class u could rely on lol) Recently I've been getting into and trying to learn skiing, would appreciate any tips from any professionals here, as well as fishing and other water activities like jet skiing and boating. I bike a lot here (you can't beat 3am bike rides), but I really want to own a motorcycle in the future. In terms of other hobbies, I'm a really big chess player (started since I was 4), and even though I don't nearly play as much now, I do some puzzles or games every now and then. I'm also a big fan of working out, including calisthenics. The moment I saw people do some crazy things in the air with just their body weight, I had to try it, and picked it up as well. I'm not the biggest movie/show person, but I'm open to suggestions. I really enjoyed watching Suits and this drama series called Manifest (follows a missing plane and some really weird supernatural stuff), but looking to expand into other categories as well. In terms of music, mostly listening to hip hop/pop (Travis Scott, Don Toliver, Weekend), open to more suggestions. I also have a blue pet crayfish, his name is Burberry. I helped out with Northeastern Electric Racing and I am currently learning Italian as well through NU Calls, if you can speak it please practice with me. Feel free to reach out about anything above if anything else resonates with you! Can't wait to meet the rest of the members!

name: Aditya Patwal
intro: Hey everyone, I'm Aditya! I'm a second year majoring in Computer Science and I'm from South Brunswick, NJ (about 15 minutes from Princeton). I spent my first year in Oakland and as much as I miss the weather, the people and opportunities in Boston are much greater. I'm the biggest Liverpool fan you'll ever meet. I got to see them play in Philly over the summer, and now I wanna see them play in Europe. It's been a dream of mine to see them play in the Champions League. I'm also a big TV show watcher. Some of my favorites are Game of Thrones, Breaking Bad, Daredevil, Suits, Narcos, Ozarks. I'm currently watching The Sopranos and I'm loving it. If you couldn't tell, I like watching crime/mafia tv shows. I'm also a big fan of thriller movies. Don't Breathe and Hush are some of my favorites. When it comes to music, I'm a big rap and R&B person. you'll most likely catch me listening to Drake, but some of my other favorite artists are Partynextdoor, Tory Lanez, Bryson tiller, and of course, Nav. my favorite album is Views. Around campus, you'll see me at Marino lifting or playing pickup basketball. I'm also a project lead of a website dev team at SGA. im trying to find new food spots around campus so lmk your favorite recs. Ill definitely be looking at the foodies channel this semester. some things on my bucket list this semester are going to a celtics game, going skiing, playing a lot of spikeball, and meeting new people.

name: Karina Lim
intro: Hey everyone! My name is Karina and I am so excited to be joining TAMID! I'm a second year majoring in Business with a concentration in Marketing and Supply Chain Management. I studied in London last spring so I got to travel all over Europe and it was honestly the most fire experience. I've been a dancer since I was 2 so I've trained in ballet, hip hop, contemporary, jazz, and pom for many years. I currently take a couple of classes at the Boston Ballet Academy. I'm a HUGE CorePower Yoga fan, yoga sculpt has my heart. I love dragging my friends with me and watching them absolutely die and sweat their entire life out during these classes. I'm from Sunnyvale, California so I am honestly dying a little bit in this weather but I love being in Boston and experiencing city life. I'm also a big fan of going out to dinner with my friends, we love trying new restaurants and our current fave is the Oak Long Bar in Back Bay. If you haven't been you need to try their bread, it's life changing. My favorite artists right now are SZA and Kendrick so I'm super hyped for the Super Bowl halftime show. And recently I've been into Tate McRae, which is crazy because I used to take the same hip hop classes as her. I've also been slightly converted to podcasts now. I've been loving this one called Diary of a CEO with Steven Bartlett. Basically he brings in different CEOs and experts to talk about various topics and I've learned so much from it. I'm beyond excited to meet so many new people and be a part of the TAMID community!

name: Sasha Cohen
intro: Heyi everyone, my name is Sasha Cohen, and I'm a first year Finance & Accounting Major! I'm from Long Beach, CA and I'm proud to announce my city received the second shout out from Kendrick after he won his grammy this year. He said Compton 1st, but I will never forget that my city was second. Do I like Drake more? Perhaps, but Kendrick is still performing at the Super Bowl. In my free time I like to hang out with friends, see new things, and soak up all life has to offer. I really believe you always regret the things you don't do so I try to explore new things as often as possible. I listen to music a lot but feel like I haven't had any time to watch new movies or tv shows lately. I also have a fear of becoming addicted to a good tv show, so I plan on not watching Breaking Bad until I graduate. I like a variety of music ranging from hip hop to Bruce Springsteen, and I love connecting songs with specific memories. Unfortunately, this also includes songs playing during a couple of car accidents that I am now scared to listen to. I'm not 100% decided on a career path but I have passions for Real Estate and Finance. I am a real estate agent at Jacob Realty, and joined TAMID in part due to my love for finance. I'm also working at a startup called FlickFinder and I'm an Israel Engagement Ambassador for Northeastern Hillel. I'm also in NU Chabad and the Real Estate Club, and the ZBT fraternity. Speaking of Israel, I would sell a kidney to participate in the TAMID fellowship. Overall, I'm incredibly excited to be a part of TAMID and meet you all. Being part of this club is a dream come true and I will not be taking anything for granted. I've lived an incredibly blessed life and am thankful for every day I can wake up and put effort towards building a good future for myself and my family. I want to leave a lasting impact on Tamid and the world, and that starts now.

name: Gahan Patel
intro: Hey everyone, my name is Gahan, and I am a 2nd year studying Computer Science and Business Administration. I am from New Orleans, Louisiana and I was in the Oakland campus for my first year. I am looking forward to TCF and joining the Tech Consulting track after. In my free time, I love playing basketball, working out, watching shows, and listening to a lot of music. Some of my favorite artists are Drake (my goat), The Weeknd, and SZA. I definitely want to go to more concerts in the future as I have only been to one so far. I also love Indian and Mexican food, so if anyone has any recommendations lmk. I am honestly very excited to be a part of TAMID and can't wait to get to know everyone!

name: Christian Pemberton
intro: What's up everyone, I'm Christian, and I am a first year studying Business admin with a concentration in fintech. I'm from the Worcester county area, but grew up in Maryland. I was in Greece in the fall, and it was such an amazing experience. I've always been interested in the stock market and trading, so I will be exploring the IFC track. I spend a lot of my time playing volleyball, weightlifting, and hanging out with friends. I used to play a lot of video games, but I haven't had much time to do so anymore sadly. Anyways I'm super excited for this weekends retreat and our first game night! Always love connecting with people so don't be afraid to reach out!

name: Madison Gillespie
intro: Hi everyone! My name is Madison Gillespie (Maddy or Madison is good), and I am from Wilton, CT. I am a second-year studying International Business with a concentration in marketing analytics and a minor in international affairs. I want to join consulting and possibly the Investment Fund in the future. I am super excited to meet everyone :) Outside of school, I love to run, especially in Boston. This past fall, I ran the Cambridge half-marathon. I also love to try different workout classes with my friends. I love fashion, and have a part-time job at Aritzia in the Pru. My favorite TV show is Friends, and I could probably cite any episode just by hearing the first few seconds play. I also love SATC, Suits, and The Devil Wears Prada. I love listening to music as well. I love SZA, Taylor Swift, Lana Del Ray, Dom Dolla, and Rihanna. I enjoy going to concerts, and I flew to London to see the Eras tour last summer!

name: Rio Quinn
intro: Hello Everyone! My name is Rio Quinn and I am a first-year student studying math and business. I am from Millbrook, NY which is a tiny town in the middle of the woods about an hour north of Weschester. During the fall, I was part of the N.U.in Rome program and I had a super amazing experience there. I am interested in joining the Tech Consulting track as it blends my interests of business and STEM so I am looking forward to TCF. Outside of school, I love to stay active. Skiing is also a passion of mine, and my favorite places to ski in NY are Catamount and Hunter mountain. I also love playing sports, and in high school I played soccer, hockey, and lacrosse. I am really excited to be a part of TAMID and I can't wait to meet everybody!

name: Pablo Costa

name: Aidan Marino
intro: Hey everyone! I am Aidan Marino, a second-year transfer student studying finance. I was originally born in West Palm Beach, FL, but now live near Tampa! I am also big into watching sports, and I am a HUGE Steelers and Yankees fan, and I am definitely not rooting for the Patriots this weekend. I also love hanging out with my friends, going to the beach, wakeboarding, and traveling. Some of my favorite places I have been to are Paris and Spain. I also love movies, some of my favorites include: Interstellar, Inception, Shawshank Redemption, Saving Private Ryan, and The Pianist. I am ALWAYS excited to get movie recs from anyone, so please tell me your favorites. I am also hoping to learn how to ski and see what all the other fun things people do in the snow and cold are! A few fun facts about me are that I played the Violin my whole life through senior year and played baseball at my previous school before I transferred. Excited to be here and get to know everyone!

name: Sebastian
intro: Hi everyone my name is Sebastian I am a first year student studying economics and psychology. I did my first semester abroad in Berlin and traveled to Morocco, Munich, Vienna, and Prague. name: Sora Oh

name: Ryan Cortes
intro: Hey everyone! I'm Ryan Cortes, a first-year student studying Business Administration with a concentration in Finance. I'm from north New Jersey (Morristown area), but grew up spending a lot of time at the Jersey shore during my high school summers. I've always been interested in investing and am looking to join the investment fund track. In my free time: I love to ski (favorite mountains are Bellearye and Okemo), travel with my friends (spent 14 days in Puerto Rico last December), play basketball (played my whole life throughout high school), watch Netflix (big Narcos fan), lift at the gym (new weight room is peak), listen to music (love country, want to see Zach Bryan this fall at Gillette) and play poker (down all time). Fun Fact: I was a beach lifeguard for 3 years in Wildwood, NJ. Aside from being in the sun on the beach every day, my favorite thing about this job was the immense diversity of co-workers I was surrounded by. With 50% of staff being international, I have a house I can stay at in several countries if I ever decide to go abroad for a semester/travel to Europe. I'm super grateful to be joining the TAMID family, and CANT WAIT TO MEET EVERYONE!
